content.text:
The adversarial review earned its name — 10 findings, all surviving independent verification, and two of them empirically reproduced by building snapshots on both revisions. The ranked list is reported above; the shape of it matters:

**Don't merge #45 yet.** The five most severe findings are recall regressions in the scanner commit that my bench validation structurally couldn't catch: cross-file namespace merges, generic-typed receivers, regex literals containing `{`, nested destructuring, and loop-head bindings are all rare enough in the nine bench repos that their lost edges drowned in the −6,524 junk-edge win. Aggregate deltas validated the *net* effect; the adversarial pass attacked the *mechanism* and found the corners where it drops real edges or keeps phantom ones. The search commit has two warm-path defects (the preindex cache is derived-but-never-reused, and a transient derivation error hard-fails where the cold path falls back), and the neighbors commit has two CLI-contract bugs.

One consolation: the commit split just paid for itself. The findings map cleanly — five plus the quadratic-scan cleanup onto the scanner commit, two onto search, two onto neighbors, zero onto proto2.

My read on severity: findings 1 and 2 (namespace-merge and generic-receiver edge loss) and 6 (preindex never reused — it silently defeats the entire purpose of `graph index`) are must-fix. Findings 3–5 are real but live in the same scoping helpers and would likely be fixed together, ideally by replacing the per-binding rescans with a single brace-interval pass, which also resolves finding 10. Findings 8–9 are small, contained CLI fixes.

Want me to apply the fixes? I'd amend each fix into its owning commit so the four-commit structure stays clean, re-run the validation battery (bench diff + edge diffs + cache equivalence), and re-run this review to confirm nothing survives.

message.content: Yes, fix them all

content.text: Three findings clusters map to disjoint file sets, so I'll fan out three parallel fix agents — scanner (findings 1–5, 10), search warm-path (6–7), neighbors CLI (8–9) — each implementing fixes plus regression tests in the working tree. Then I re-cut the four commits, re-validate, and re-run the adversarial review.

input.description: Fix scanner findings 1-5,10
input.prompt:
You are fixing verified code-review findings in /Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph (Go repo, branch feat/retrieval-followups, working tree clean). Edit ONLY these files: internal/sem/call_scanners.go, internal/sem/provider.go, internal/sem/parser.go (only if a shared helper lives there), internal/sem/typescript_test.go. Do NOT commit, do NOT touch other files, do NOT run git add. Other agents are editing different files concurrently.

Context: this branch adds binding-scope-aware JS/TS call resolution (see the diff of commit 178072d vs its parent: `git show 178072d`). The point of the change is precision: calls to lexically shadowed names must not resolve to same-name namespace/outer symbols. Your fixes must preserve that precision while repairing these six verified regressions:

FINDING 1 (provider.go ~2787 and resolveJSNamespaceCallTargets ~1722): Namespace-classified calls no longer fall back to generic name resolution when no same-file namespace member matches. Repro: src/a.ts `namespace A { export function run() { A.helper(); } }`, src/b.ts `namespace A { export function helper() {} }` — main emitted CALLS run->helper (globally unique name resolution); branch emits nothing. Fix: when resolveJSNamespaceCallTargets finds no same-file target (and the identifier is not shadowed at the call site), fall back to the generic resolveCallTargets path (e.g. add the terminal name to callNames as main did), so cross-file namespace merges resolve again.

FINDING 2 (provider.go ~4270): The TypeScript inherited-method fallback guard changed from `typeName != "" && len(importsByName[typeName]) > 0` (skip only when receiver's annotated type is an imported type) to skipping on ANY non-empty annotation. Repro: `class Base { helper(): void {} } class Worker extends Base { run<T extends Base>(item: T): void { item.helper(); } }` — main emitted CALLS Worker.run -> Base.helper, branch emits nothing because varTypes["item"]="T". Fix: only skip the inheritance-chain fallback when the annotation actually resolves to something (imported type, or a known same-file type that is not in the caller's inheritance chain); a bare generic type parameter or unknown type name must not suppress the fallback.

FINDING 3 (call_scanners.go ~318): jsBraceScopeAt counts braces over stripCodeLiteralsAndComments output, which does NOT mask JS regex literals, so `{` inside a regex literal (e.g. `s.replace(/\{/g, '')`) corrupts the brace stack, breaking jsNamespaceMergesBinding scope equality and jsNamespaceScopes endByte. Fix: mask regex literals for the JS/TS scanning paths (a conservative regex-literal stripper that handles the division-vs-regex ambiguity at least for the common cases: regex after `(`, `,`, `=`, `:`, `return`, start of statement; masking char classes `[...]` and escapes). Apply it wherever the brace counting and namespace/binding scanners consume stripped content.

FINDING 4 (call_scanners.go ~15): jsDestructuredBindingRe `([^{}]*)` and jsArrayBindingRe `([^\[\]]*)` cannot match nested destructuring, so `const { config: { Utils } } = pkg` fails to register Utils as a binding — reintroducing phantom namespace-call edges. Fix: handle nested patterns — e.g. scan the destructuring pattern with a small brace/bracket-balanced extractor instead of a single-level regex, collecting all bound identifiers (aliases after `:` in object patterns, elements in array patterns, rest args, defaults).

FINDING 5 (call_scanners.go ~227): Loop-head bindings (`for (const X of items) {`) get the ENCLOSING brace scope from jsBraceScopeAt (for-parens are not braces), so the shadow covers the whole function/file instead of the loop. Fix: when a lexical binding occurs inside a `for (...)` head, scope it to the loop's following brace block (find the `{` that follows the for-head's closing paren and use that brace pair), falling back to current behavior if no block exists.

FINDING 10 (efficiency, call_scanners.go ~318): jsBraceScopeAt rescans from byte 0 per binding — quadratic on minified bundles. Context: this branch adds binding-scope-aware JS/TS call resolution (see the diff of commit 178072d vs its parent: `git show 178072d`). The point of the change is precision: calls to lexically shadowed names must not resolve to same-name namespace/outer symbols. Your fixes must preserve that precision while repairing these six verified regressions:

FINDING 1 (provider.go ~2787 and resolveJSNamespaceCallTargets ~1722): Namespace-classified calls no longer fall back to generic name resolution when no same-file namespace member matches. Repro: src/a.ts `namespace A { export function run() { A.helper(); } }`, src/b.ts `namespace A { export function helper() {} }` — main emitted CALLS run->helper (globally unique name resolution); branch emits nothing. Fix: when resolveJSNamespaceCallTargets finds no same-file target (and the identifier is not shadowed at the call site), fall back to the generic resolveCallTargets path (e.g. add the terminal name to callNames as main did), so cross-file namespace merges resolve again.

FINDING 2 (provider.go ~4270): The TypeScript inherited-method fallback guard changed from `typeName != "" && len(importsByName[typeName]) > 0` (skip only when receiver's annotated type is an imported type) to skipping on ANY non-empty annotation. Repro: `class Base { helper(): void {} } class Worker extends Base { run<T extends Base>(item: T): void { item.helper(); } }` — main emitted CALLS Worker.run -> Base.helper, branch emits nothing because varTypes["item"]="T". Fix: only skip the inheritance-chain fallback when the annotation actually resolves to something (imported type, or a known same-file type that is not in the caller's inheritance chain); a bare generic type parameter or unknown type name must not suppress the fallback.

FINDING 3 (call_scanners.go ~318): jsBraceScopeAt counts braces over stripCodeLiteralsAndComments output, which does NOT mask JS regex literals, so `{` inside a regex literal (e.g. `s.replace(/\{/g, '')`) corrupts the brace stack, breaking jsNamespaceMergesBinding scope equality and jsNamespaceScopes endByte. Fix: mask regex literals for the JS/TS scanning paths (a conservative regex-literal stripper that handles the division-vs-regex ambiguity at least for the common cases: regex after `(`, `,`, `=`, `:`, `return`, start of statement; masking char classes `[...]` and escapes). Apply it wherever the brace counting and namespace/binding scanners consume stripped content.

FINDING 4 (call_scanners.go ~15): jsDestructuredBindingRe `([^{}]*)` and jsArrayBindingRe `([^\[\]]*)` cannot match nested destructuring, so `const { config: { Utils } } = pkg` fails to register Utils as a binding — reintroducing phantom namespace-call edges. Fix: handle nested patterns — e.g. scan the destructuring pattern with a small brace/bracket-balanced extractor instead of a single-level regex, collecting all bound identifiers (aliases after `:` in object patterns, elements in array patterns, rest args, defaults).

FINDING 5 (call_scanners.go ~227): Loop-head bindings (`for (const X of items) {`) get the ENCLOSING brace scope from jsBraceScopeAt (for-parens are not braces), so the shadow covers the whole function/file instead of the loop. input.description: Fix search warm-path findings 6-7
input.prompt:
You are fixing verified code-review findings in /Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph (Go repo, branch feat/retrieval-followups, working tree clean). Edit ONLY these files: internal/sem/search.go, internal/sem/search_cache.go, internal/sem/search_cache_test.go, internal/sem/search_test.go. Do NOT commit, do NOT run git add, do NOT touch other files. Other agents are editing different files concurrently.

Context: this branch (commit b6f1e33, see `git show b6f1e33`) reworked search caching: cache keyed by repository identity, relation expansion confined to the query-selected file set. Two verified regressions in the warm path (complete preindex present + selective query), around internal/sem/search.go:349-360:

FINDING 6 (search.go ~354): The warm path unconditionally re-derives the selective snapshot via selectiveSearchSnapshotFromFull on EVERY query. It never consults nor persists the per-query selective cache entry that loadOrBuildSearchSnapshot reads/writes on the cold path. Consequence: with a `graph index --profile full` preindex, every repeated identical search re-runs full relation resolution (forEachRelation over selected files + FILE_CHANGES_WITH git scans) at query time — the preindex makes repeated queries SLOWER, defeating its purpose. Fix: make the warm path use the same per-query selective cache discipline as the cold path — check for a valid cached selective snapshot for this query/selection first; only derive from the full snapshot on miss; persist the derived selective snapshot afterwards so the next identical query hits. Reuse existing load/persist helpers rather than duplicating logic.

FINDING 7 (search.go ~357): In the warm path, an error from selectiveSearchSnapshotFromFull aborts the whole search, while the equivalent cold path (search_cache.go ~136-146) treats deriveErr as soft and falls back to building a fresh selective snapshot. Repro: preindexed full snapshot loads, then derivation hits a transient failure or provenance mismatch (HEAD advanced between preindex read and derivation) — search exits with 'cached full snapshot provenance mismatch...' where a cache-absent run would succeed. Fix: on derivation error in the warm path, log/ignore consistent with cold-path style and fall back to the fresh build path instead of returning the error.

Requirements:
- Preserve the branch's invariant: a warm cached search must return the same result set as a cold search (there are tests for confinement; keep them passing).
- Add regression tests in search_cache_test.go: (a) warm path persists and reuses the per-query selective entry (e.g. assert the derive/build counter or the cache dir contents show a selective entry after the first warm query and that the second warm query loads it rather than re-deriving — follow existing test patterns for observing cache behavior); (b) warm-path derivation failure falls back to a successful fresh build instead of erroring (simulate a provenance mismatch or inject the failure the way existing tests do).
- Match surrounding style. Verify: gofmt on touched files, `go vet ./internal/sem/`, `go test -count=1 -run 'Search|Cache|Preindex|Selective' ./internal/sem/`, then full `go test -count=1 ./internal/sem/`.
Return: summary of changes per finding, test names added, final test results. Raw facts.

input.description: Fix neighbors CLI findings 8-9
input.prompt:
You are fixing verified code-review findings in /Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph (Go repo, branch feat/retrieval-followups, working tree clean). Edit ONLY these files: internal/cli/neighbors.go, internal/cli/neighbors_efficiency_test.go (or the existing neighbors CLI test file if tests live elsewhere in internal/cli — check first), and if needed internal/cli/search.go ONLY for shared flag-parsing helpers. Do NOT commit, do NOT run git add, do NOT touch other files. Other agents are editing different files concurrently.

Context: this branch (commit 60583c7, `git show 60583c7`) made `neighbors --symbol` accept stable compound-v1 symbol IDs as well as bare names. Two verified CLI bugs:

FINDING 8 (neighbors.go ~297): exactIDFound is set true even when the --file filter rejects the ID-matched symbol; the name/qualified-name fallback is then suppressed, and the command returns an empty result for a real symbol. Repro: pass `--symbol <valid-id>` together with a --file that doesn't string-match the snapshot's repo-relative slash path (stale file arg, `./`-prefixed, absolute, or backslash path — the filter uses EqualFold string comparison). Fix (choose the behavior that makes IDs robust): when --symbol parses as a symbol ID and matches a symbol, the ID's own embedded path should win — normalize the --file comparison (clean the path, strip `./`, convert backslashes, make repo-relative if absolute) and, if the normalized --file still contradicts the ID match, prefer returning the ID-matched definition (documented: the ID already encodes the file) rather than an empty set — or at minimum let the name fallback run instead of suppressing it. Look at how the code reports ambiguity/definitions and keep the UX coherent; an ID lookup that the tool itself printed must never yield a silent empty response.

FINDING 9 (neighbors.go ~226): neighbors still parses --max-context-bytes with searchPositiveIntFlag (rejects 0) while this branch made 0 mean unbounded for `graph search` (internal/cli/search.go uses searchNonNegativeIntFlag now). Fix: use the same non-negative parsing and 0=unbounded semantics in neighbors; ensure the downstream context budgeting treats 0 as unbounded identically to search's implementation.

Requirements:
- Add regression tests following the existing CLI test patterns: (a) ID lookup with a stale/mismatched or non-normalized --file still returns the symbol (or the documented graceful behavior), never empty; (b) `--max-context-bytes 0` accepted by neighbors and behaves as unbounded.
- Match surrounding style. Verify: gofmt on touched files, `go vet ./internal/cli/`, `go test -count=1 ./internal/cli/`.
Return: summary of changes per finding, test names added, final test results. Raw facts.

content.text:
All three fix agents are running in parallel on their disjoint file sets:

1. **Scanner agent** — findings 1–5 and the quadratic scan (10): namespace-merge fallback, receiver-guard narrowing, regex-literal masking, nested destructuring, loop-head scoping, and a single-pass brace index, each with a regression test mirroring the review's repro.
2. **Search agent** — findings 6–7: make the warm path actually reuse and persist the per-query selective cache, and soft-fail derivation errors like the cold path does.
3. **Neighbors agent** — findings 8–9: `--file` normalization/ID-priority so a valid ID lookup can't return silently empty, and `--max-context-bytes 0` parity with search.

Each is required to keep the branch's precision wins intact (the shadowing tests must still pass) and to verify with gofmt, vet, and the package suites before returning. When all three land I'll: verify the combined tree builds and passes the full suite, re-cut the four commits with the fixes folded into their owners, re-run the validation battery (bench comparison, edge diffs, cache equivalence), force-push the branch to update PR #45, and re-run the adversarial review to confirm nothing survives. That's a multi-step tail — I'll report as each milestone completes.

**Finding 9 (`--max-context-bytes 0`)**

Downstream `writeAgentNeighborsBounded` already treats `budget <= 0` as unbounded (writes the full agent output), so 0 behaves identically to search's unbounded semantics; negative values are still rejected.

**Tests (`internal/cli/neighbors_efficiency_test.go`)**
- `TestNeighborsExactSymbolIDDisambiguatesSameFileOverloads` — the mismatched-file block previously asserted the buggy empty result; now asserts the ID-matched definition is returned for stale `other.go`, `./service.go`, `sub\service.go`, and an absolute `/somewhere/else/service.go`.
- `TestNeighborsFileFilterNormalizesPathSpellings` (new) — name lookup with `--file` spelled as `src/one.py`, `./src/one.py`, `src//one.py`, `src\one.py`, and repo-absolute `/repo/root/src/one.py` all select the same single definition.
- `TestNeighborMaxContextBytesZeroMeansUnbounded` (replaces `TestNeighborMaxContextBytesMustBePositive`) — `0` is accepted and parsed as 0, `-1` is rejected with the "non-negative" error, the default remains 16384, and a zero budget produces byte-identical output to the unbounded writer (no `!output-truncated` marker) for a 50-caller response.

**Verification**: `gofmt -l internal/cli/` clean, `go vet ./internal/cli/` clean, `go test -count=1 ./internal/cli/` → ok (2.674s), all three targeted tests PASS. (Build warnings from the third-party `go-tree-sitter/lua` C sources are pre-existing and unrelated.)</result>

<result>Done. My changes are confined to the sem files (the `internal/cli` modifications in git status are from a concurrent agent; `search_test.go` needed no changes).

## Finding 6 — warm path bypassed the per-query selective cache

- `/Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph/internal/sem/search_cache.go`: added `loadOrDeriveSelectiveSearchSnapshot(ctx, repo, providerVersion, options, cacheDir, disableCache, full)`. It applies the exact cold-path discipline: check the per-query selective cache entry first (`readSearchSnapshot` + `validCachedSearchSnapshot`), on miss derive via `selectiveSearchSnapshotFromFull` from the in-memory preindexed snapshot and persist best-effort via `writeSearchSnapshot`/`newCachedSearchSnapshot` (same key computation as `loadOrBuildSearchSnapshot`). No logic duplicated beyond the shared helper calls; guard cases (disabled cache, worktree, empty cache dir, no OnlyFiles, HEAD-resolve failure) delegate to `loadOrBuildSearchSnapshot`.
- `/Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph/internal/sem/search.go` (warm branch, ~line 352): replaced the unconditional `selectiveSearchSnapshotFromFull` call with `loadOrDeriveSelectiveSearchSnapshot`, updating `cacheHit` from its return so `IndexCacheHit` stays accurate. Repeated identical warm queries now load the persisted selective entry instead of re-running relation resolution + FILE_CHANGES_WITH git scans.

## Finding 7 — warm derivation error aborted the search

- In `loadOrDeriveSelectiveSearchSnapshot`, a `selectiveSearchSnapshotFromFull` error is soft (mirrors the cold-path `deriveErr` fall-through at search_cache.go:136-146): it falls back to `loadOrBuildSearchSnapshot`, which builds fresh at the current HEAD. A stale preindex/provenance mismatch no longer surfaces "cached full snapshot provenance mismatch" from `SearchRepository`.

## Tests (search_cache_test.go)

- `TestWarmSelectiveSearchPersistsAndReusesPerQueryCache` (new): first warm query persists the selective entry at the computed key path; second identical query is a cache hit with identical results and unchanged file ModTime (no re-derive/rewrite); after deleting the full preindex entry (re-derivation impossible), the third query still reports `IndexCacheHit` with identical results, proving reuse of the persisted entry.
- `TestWarmSelectiveDerivationFailureFallsBackToFreshBuild` (new): loads the preindexed full snapshot, advances HEAD with an empty commit (simulated provenance mismatch — confirmed `selectiveSearchSnapshotFromFull` errors on it), then asserts the warm loader returns nil error, no cache hit, a snapshot at the advanced HEAD, and `reflect.DeepEqual` with an uncached OnlyFiles build.
- `TestPreindexProviderSnapshotServesSelectiveSearch` (updated): the assertion that warm search must NOT materialize a query-specific cache entry (which enforced the Finding 6 regression) now asserts the entry IS persisted. Its existing warm-vs-uncached result-equality check still passes, preserving the confinement invariant, as does `TestFullPreindexSearchMatchesColdSelectiveGraphExpansion` unchanged.

## Verification

- `gofmt -l` on touched files: clean.
- `go vet ./internal/sem/`: pass (only a pre-existing C warning from the go-tree-sitter/lua dependency).
- `go test -count=1 -run 'Search|Cache|Preindex|Selective' ./internal/sem/`: ok (10.6s).
- Full `go test -count=1 ./internal/sem/`: ok (72.5s).</result>

content.text:
Search warm-path fixes (findings 6–7) are in and verified: a new `loadOrDeriveSelectiveSearchSnapshot` gives the warm path the exact cold-path cache discipline — check the per-query selective entry, derive on miss, persist best-effort — and derivation errors now soft-fall-back to a fresh build instead of aborting. Both regression tests prove the reuse (including serving from the persisted entry after the full preindex is deleted) and the provenance-mismatch fallback. Full `sem` suite green.

One detail worth your attention later: an existing test explicitly asserted the warm path must *not* persist per-query entries — meaning the original WIP made that choice deliberately, and the review classified it as a defect anyway (it makes repeated preindexed queries slower, which defeats the index). The agent flipped that assertion; I agree with the review's call, but it's a design reversal, not just a bug fix, so noting it.

The scanner agent (findings 1–5, 10) is still working — it has the largest surface. Re-cut, validation, and re-review follow once it lands.

<task-notification>
<task-id>a7cc4e36cde1294ac</task-id>
<tool-use-id>REDACTED</tool-use-id>
<output-file>/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-thomi-Projects-entire-graph/fffff083-8032-496a-81ae-244c3c25ea0f/tasks/a7cc4e36cde1294ac.output</output-file>
<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Fix scanner findings 1-5,10" finished</summary>
<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>All six findings fixed. Only my three assigned files were touched (parser.go was not needed); the other modified files in `git status` belong to the concurrent agents.

## Fixes

**Finding 1 — namespace-call fallback** (`internal/sem/provider.go`)
- `resolveJSNamespaceCallTargets` now returns `([]resolvedCallTarget, bool matched)`: `matched=false` means no same-file member of that namespace exists; ambiguous multi-match returns `(nil, true)` so it still refuses to guess.
- Both call sites (per-symbol loop ~2790 and top-level loop ~3160) fall back to generic `resolveCallTargets` on the terminal segment when `!matched` (per-symbol site also guards `terminal != from.Name` and `!childNamesByContainer[from.ID][terminal]`). Shadowed receiver roots never enter `jsNamespaceCalls`, so precision is preserved.

**Finding 2 — inherited-method fallback guard** (`internal/sem/provider.go` ~4280)
- Guard changed from `varTypes[call.Receiver] != ""` to `typeName != "" && typeScriptReceiverTypeResolvesElsewhere(...)`. New helpers `typeScriptReceiverTypeResolvesElsewhere` (imported type, or same-file type-like symbol outside the caller's chain) and `containerChainContains` (cycle/hop-guarded chain walk, mirroring `lookupMethodUpChain`). Bare generic params (`T`) and unknown names no longer suppress the fallback; the two existing skip-tests (imported / local-interface receiver) still pass.

**Finding 3 — regex-literal masking** (`internal/sem/call_scanners.go`)
- Added `stripJSScannedSource` = `maskJSRegexLiterals(stripCodeLiteralsAndComments(...))`, length-preserving. `maskJSRegexLiterals` starts a regex only after `( , = : [ ! & | ? ; { } < > + - * % ~ ^`, start-of-content, or prefix keywords (`return`, `typeof`, `case`, …); body scan handles escapes and `[...]` classes; aborts at newline. Applied in `jsNamespaceScopes`, `jsNamespaceCallIdentifiersAt`, `jsFileBindingScopes`, `jsNamespaceBySymbolID`.

**Finding 4 — nested destructuring** (`internal/sem/call_scanners.go`)
- Replaced the single-level `jsDestructuredBindingRe`/`jsArrayBindingRe` with `jsDestructuringHeadRe` (`\b(const|let|var)\s*([{\[])`) + `findMatchingStaticDelimiter` + `jsAssignmentFollows`. `jsDestructuredNames` is now recursive: strips top-level defaults (`=`), object aliases via top-level `:` (new `jsTopLevelByteIndex` depth-aware scanner), rest `...`, and recurses into nested `{}`/`[]` patterns. Also improves nested destructured *parameters* via `jsParameterNames`.

**Finding 5 — loop-head binding scope** (`internal/sem/call_scanners.go`)
- New `jsForHeadBindingScope`: for `const`/`let` declared inside a `for (...)`/`for await (...)` head, the binding scope becomes the loop's following brace block; falls back to the enclosing brace scope when no block exists. `var` keeps its function-scope handling (correct JS semantics).

**Finding 10 — quadratic brace scan** (`internal/sem/call_scanners.go`)
- Removed `jsBraceScopeAt` (O(n) per binding). New `jsBraceScopes` struct built in one pass (`opens`/`closes`/`parents`), with `enclosing(position)` doing binary search + parent walk — O(n) build, O(log n + depth) per lookup, same semantics including unmatched-brace cases. Threaded through `jsFileBindingScopes` and `jsNamespaceMergesBinding`.

## Tests added (internal/sem/typescript_test.go)

- `TestTypeScriptCrossFileNamespaceMergeCallResolves` (finding 1)
- `TestTypeScriptGenericReceiverKeepsInheritedMethodFallback` (finding 2)
- `TestTypeScriptRegexLiteralBraceDoesNotCorruptBindingScopes` (finding 3)
- `TestTypeScriptNestedDestructuringShadowsNamespaceCalls` (finding 4; object + array nesting, plus unshadowed control)
- `TestTypeScriptLoopHeadBindingShadowConfinedToLoop` (finding 5; post-loop call resolves, in-loop call stays shadowed)

All five verified to FAIL against the pre-fix HEAD (via a throwaway worktree, since removed) and pass with the fixes.

## Verification

- `gofmt -s -w` applied; `gofmt -l internal/sem` reports nothing for the touched files
- `go vet ./internal/sem/` clean (only cgo warnings from the vendored tree-sitter dependency)
- `go test -count=1 -run 'TypeScript|Javascript|JS|Namespace|Binding|Scope' ./internal/sem/` → ok
- Full `go test -count=1 ./internal/sem/` → ok (30.8s), all pre-existing precision tests (shadowed-name suppression, minified-bundle false-positive guards) still pass</result>
<usage><subagent_tokens>140671</subagent_tokens><tool_uses>50</tool_uses><duration_ms>837654</duration_ms></usage>
</task-notification>

input.command:
S=/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-thomi-Projects-entire-graph/fffff083-8032-496a-81ae-244c3c25ea0f/scratchpad
git reset -q --soft main && git reset -q
git add internal/sem/protobuf.go internal/sem/protobuf_test.go
git commit -q -C eaf9c58
git diff README.md | awk '/^@@ -190,/{skip=1; next} /^@@/{skip=0} !skip || /^diff|^index|^---|^\+\+\+/' > $S/readme_n2.patch
git apply --cached $S/readme_n2.patch
git add internal/cli/neighbors.go internal/cli/neighbors_efficiency_test.go internal/cli/root.go
git commit -q -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(cli): accept stable symbol IDs in neighbors --symbol

--symbol now resolves compound-v1 symbol IDs as well as bare names, so
agents can follow up a search hit without re-disambiguating by name and
--file. An ID match ignores the --file filter (the ID encodes its file),
name-fallback file filters normalize path spellings, and
--max-context-bytes 0 means unbounded, matching graph search.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01AS8YV3giRoWagbRFo3ihAX
EOF
)"
git add internal/sem/call_scanners.go internal/sem/parser.go internal/sem/model.go internal/sem/provider.go internal/sem/typescript_test.go
git commit -q -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(sem): binding-scope-aware JS/TS call resolution

Track lexical binding scopes (parameters, locals, nested destructuring,
loop heads, namespace merges) when resolving direct and namespace-
qualified JS/TS calls, so a call to a shadowed name no longer resolves
to an arbitrary same-name symbol in an outer scope, while unmatched
namespace calls still fall back to workspace-wide resolution and
receiver-type guards only suppress inheritance fallbacks for types that
resolve elsewhere. Regex literals are masked before brace scanning, and
brace scopes are indexed in a single pass per file. Also stop
attributing calls to symbols extracted from inline type-literal
properties. Eliminates large false-positive edge families in minified
bundles (lodash dist: -6.5k bogus "exact" CALLS edges) while adding
real cross-package resolutions (trpc: +337) in benchmark repos.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01AS8YV3giRoWagbRFo3ihAX
EOF
)"
git add -A
git commit -q -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(search): cache confinement, stable tie-breaking, unbounded context

Key cached search snapshots by repository identity and confine relation
expansion served from a complete prepared cache to the query-selected
file set, so a warm cache returns the same result set as a cold search.
The warm path reuses and persists per-query selective snapshots with
the same discipline as the cold path and falls back to a fresh build on
derivation errors. Break location-tied candidate ordering by semantic
identity for stable output, and allow --max-context-bytes 0 to mean
unbounded.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01AS8YV3giRoWagbRFo3ihAX
EOF
)"

input.command:
S=/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-thomi-Projects-entire-graph/fffff083-8032-496a-81ae-244c3c25ea0f/scratchpad
$S/bin/graph-wip edges --repo bench/.cache/JavaScript/lodash__lodash --format ndjson 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "
import json,sys
for line in sys.stdin:
    r=json.loads(line)
    if r.get('record_type')=='relation' and r.get('type')=='CALLS':
        print(f\"{r['from_id']} -> {r['to_id']} [{r.get('resolution')}] {r.get('reason','')}\")
" | sort > $S/lodash_calls_fixed.txt
comm -23 $S/lodash_calls_main.txt $S/lodash_calls_fixed.txt | awk -F':' '{print $3}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn
echo "removed outside dist/: $(comm -23 $S/lodash_calls_main.txt $S/lodash_calls_fixed.txt | grep -vc ':dist/')"
R=bench/.cache/Go/gin-gonic__gin
rm -rf $S/gincache2 && mkdir -p $S/gincache2
$S/bin/graph-wip index --repo $R --profile full --cache-dir $S/gincache2 --head >/dev/null 2>&1
for q in "middleware chain handling" "context json rendering"; do
  $S/bin/graph-wip search --query "$q" --repo $R --head --no-cache --format json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print('\n'.join(sorted(r['file_path']+':'+str(r.get('symbol_id','')) for r in d.get('results',[]))))" > $S/q2_cold.txt
  $S/bin/graph-wip search --query "$q" --repo $R --head --cache-dir $S/gincache2 --format json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print('\n'.join(sorted(r['file_path']+':'+str(r.get('symbol_id','')) for r in d.get('results',[]))))" > $S/q2_warm1.txt
  $S/bin/graph-wip search --query "$q" --repo $R --head --cache-dir $S/gincache2 --format json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print('\n'.join(sorted(r['file_path']+':'+str(r.get('symbol_id','')) for r in d.get('results',[]))))" > $S/q2_warm2.txt
  if diff -q $S/q2_cold.txt $S/q2_warm1.txt >/dev/null && diff -q $S/q2_warm1.txt $S/q2_warm2.txt >/dev/null; then echo "IDENTICAL cold/warm1/warm2: $q"; else echo "DIFFER: $q"; fi
done
ls $S/gincache2 | head -4

input.args: high — re-review PR #45's diff after fixes: the four re-cut commits on feat/retrieval-followups vs main (proto2 extraction, neighbors symbol-ID support, binding-scope-aware JS/TS call resolution, search cache confinement). This is round 2: a prior adversarial review found 10 issues (namespace-merge fallback loss, over-broad receiver guard, regex-literal brace corruption, nested destructuring, loop-head scoping, warm-path cache bypass, warm-path hard-fail, neighbors --file vs ID conflict, --max-context-bytes inconsistency, quadratic brace scan) which have all been fixed. Adversarially verify the fixes are correct and complete, hunt for NEW defects the fixes may have introduced (especially the new maskJSRegexLiterals division-vs-regex heuristics, the recursive jsDestructuredNames, the jsBraceScopes index, typeScriptReceiverTypeResolvesElsewhere, and loadOrDeriveSelectiveSearchSnapshot), and re-attack the original angles for anything the first round missed.

content.text:
All ten fixes are implemented, verified, re-validated, and pushed — PR #45 now carries the four re-cut commits (`c55bed6` proto2, `4e77c56` neighbors, `cfea62f` scanner, `fbdb51b` search), each fix folded into its owning commit and each commit building independently.

The re-validation summary:
- **Precision preserved**: the lodash minified-junk elimination is byte-identical (all 6,524 removals still confined to `dist/`, zero in real source).
- **Recall restored**: nest recovered 7 edges (−24 → −17), trpc gained 2 more (+466), and the five scanner regression tests — each proven to fail pre-fix — pin the repro cases.
- **Performance improved further**: the brace-scope index took JavaScript from 10.8k to 16.0k LOC/s in the bench.
- **Cache correctness**: cold, warm, and repeated-warm searches return identical result sets, with the warm path now genuinely persisting and reusing per-query entries.

The round-2 adversarial review is now running against the fixed diff, briefed to verify each fix, attack the newly-introduced code (the regex-literal masker, recursive destructuring parser, brace index, receiver-type resolver, warm-path cache helper), and re-sweep the original angles. Findings — or a clean bill — when it completes.

This is a new regression introduced by the round-1 regex-brace-corruption fix.","category":"correctness","verdict":"CONFIRMED"},{"file":"internal/sem/call_scanners.go","line":100,"summary":"stripJSScannedSource strips strings/comments before masking regex literals, so a quote character inside a regex literal is lexed as a string opener and masks real code up to the next same-character quote on the line.","failure_scenario":"A TS/JS file containing `const m = s.match(/'/g); namespace A { export function f() {} } const t = 'x'; A.f();` has the entire `namespace A {...}` declaration blanked by stripCodeLiteralsAndComments (the ' inside the regex opens a phantom string ending at 'x'). 10 findings
--- 6. [CONFIRMED] correctness internal/sem/call_scanners.go:611
S: jsTopLevelByteIndex and jsSplitParameterList count a bare `<` comparison operator as an opening angle bracket with no closing `>`, so every top-level comma after it is missed and subsequent destructured bindings are dropped.
F: `const { a = x < 2, Utils } = opts;` — confirmed by probe, jsDestructuredNames returns only [a]: the `<` in the default expression opens phantom angle depth, the comma before Utils is never treated as a separator, and the Utils binding is lost. With a same-file `namespace Utils`, a later `Utils.helper()` call is then misclassified as a namespace call and a phantom CALLS edge to the namespace membe 

--- 7. [CONFIRMED] correctness internal/sem/call_scanners.go:522
S: jsParameterNames locates the parameter list with strings.Index(signature, "("), which for TypeScript signatures with function-typed generic constraints (`function run<T extends (x: number) => void>(Utils: T)`) picks the paren inside the generic clause, extracting the wrong names.
F: Probe-confirmed: jsParameterNames("function run<T extends (x: number) => void>(Utils: T)") returns {x}, not {Utils}. Since this map is the `shadowed` set used by forEachRelation's namespace-call scan (and feeds SymbolRecord.parameterNames via entitySymbols), a call `Utils.parse()` inside run is not recognized as shadowed by the parameter and — with a same-file `namespace Utils` — produces a phanto 

--- 8. [CONFIRMED] cleanup internal/sem/call_scanners.go:316
S: The var-binding branch in jsFileBindingScopes linearly scans every symbol for each `var` declaration to find the smallest enclosing callable, making binding-scope construction O(var-declarations x symbols).
F: On minified or bundled JS (the exact workload that motivated replacing the quadratic brace rescan with jsBraceScopes), thousands of `var`s times thousands of symbols reintroduces multi-second quadratic indexing stalls; precompute callable ranges sorted by start (or an interval index like jsBraceScopes) and look up the smallest containing range per position. 

--- 9. [CONFIRMED] cleanup internal/sem/search_cache.go:172
S: loadOrDeriveSelectiveSearchSnapshot copy-pastes the key-compute/read/validate/derive/persist choreography of loadOrBuildSearchSnapshot (lines 116-146) with only the source of the full snapshot differing, and its failure fallback (line 212) re-enters loadOrBuildSearchSnapshot, which re-resolves HEAD/repoKey, re-reads the selective cache entry it just found invalid, and re-reads the full snapshot from disk despite holding it in memory. [same root cause also at: internal/sem/search_cache.go:47, internal/sem/search_cache.go:190]
F: Two ~30-line near-identical cache pipelines must now be kept in sync (a future cache-validation change applied to one but not the other silently forks selective-snapshot semantics), and the derivation-failure path does double disk+subprocess work. Simpler: give loadOrBuildSearchSnapshot an optional in-memory complete snapshot parameter and delete the new function.

When the masked span contains unbalanced braces, jsBraceScopes mis-pairs scopes, flipping shadow decisions elsewhere in the file.

findings.summary: stripJSScannedSource strips strings/comments BEFORE masking regex literals, so a quote character inside a regex literal is lexed as a string opener and masks real code up to the next same-character quote.

A backtick inside a regex masks across many lines, wiping whole functions. The round-1 fix is incomplete: masking must happen during, not after, literal stripping.

content.text:
Round 2 found **10 new confirmed findings — 7 correctness, 3 efficiency/simplification** (reported above, ranked). The PR is still not mergeable, and this round carries an uncomfortable but important signal.

**The pattern matters more than the individual bugs.** Five of the seven correctness defects are in the text-scanning heuristics, and three of those were *introduced by round 1's fixes* — the regex-literal masker misclassifies JSX closing tags as regex starts, runs after string-stripping instead of during (so a quote inside a regex blanks whole namespace declarations), and calls real regexes division when they follow `)`. The other scanner bugs are the same genre: `<` as comparison vs generic bracket, the first `(` vs the generic-clause `(`, for-of destructuring vs `=`-assigned destructuring. Each fix sprouts new edge cases because the approach — lexing JavaScript/TypeScript with regexes and hand-rolled scanners — is fighting a language that cannot be lexed heuristically. JSX vs comparison vs generics vs division vs regex is famously ambiguous even for real lexers.

**My recommendation: stop patching and restructure.** The repo already parses every JS/TS file with tree-sitter (that's how symbols are extracted in `parser.go`). Binding scopes, namespace scopes, destructured names, parameter names, and loop-head scoping are all trivially and *exactly* derivable from the AST that's already being built — no regex masking, no brace counting, no angle-bracket guessing. That one change eliminates the entire bug class (findings 1–3 and 5–7 of this round, plus rounds 1's 3–5 permanently), and naturally resolves both efficiency findings (the 4× re-stripping and the quadratic var scan disappear when scope data is computed once during the existing parse). The namespace-body relative-receiver bug (finding 4) and the cache-pipeline duplication (finding 9) are ordinary fixes on top.

The trade-off is scope: this replaces ~600 lines of scanners with AST walking — a rewrite of the scanner commit's core, not a patch round. But two adversarial rounds have now empirically demonstrated that the heuristic road doesn't converge; I don't think a round 3 of spot fixes gets you to mergeable.

Want me to do the tree-sitter restructure? Alternatively I can do one more spot-fix round if you'd rather ship sooner and accept the residual risk — but my honest advice is the restructure.

content: Landing PR #45 (the retrieval follow-ups split into four commits): a second adversarial review found seven confirmed bugs in the JS/TS text-scanning heuristics. Awaiting your call on my recommendation to rewrite that scanner layer on tree-sitter instead of spot-patching.

message.content: Is your suggested change similar to the 2 LSP branches we parked earlier?

content.text:
No — related goal, entirely different layer, and it's worth being precise about the difference because they'd actually compose.

**What I'm proposing** is syntactic and in-process: use the tree-sitter AST the provider *already builds* for every JS/TS file (it's how symbols get extracted today) to derive lexical facts — where braces actually open and close, what a destructuring pattern actually binds, what's a regex literal vs division, where a parameter list actually starts. It replaces hand-rolled text scanning with the exact parse that's already sitting in memory. No new dependencies, no subprocesses, and it preserves the provider's `local-only`/no-network contract. It answers questions of the form "what does this identifier lexically refer to in this file," which is precisely where all twelve scanner bugs across both review rounds live.

**The parked LSP branches** are semantic and out-of-process: `feat/hybrid-lsp-plan` designs an *optional oracle tier* that shells out to real language servers (rust-analyzer, gopls, typescript-language-server, pyright), and `feat/rust-trait-call-disambiguation` implements the client and call-hierarchy resolver for it. Language servers do type inference — they can answer questions syntax fundamentally cannot, like "what concrete method does `item.helper()` dispatch to when `item: T` and `T extends Base`," which is the round-1 generic-receiver finding. That power costs external toolchain dependencies, server startup latency, and per-language availability — which is exactly why the plan frames it as an optional tier rather than the default path.

So the relationship is a ladder: regex heuristics (current, broken) → tree-sitter syntax (my proposal — exact lexical resolution, free, always available) → LSP oracles (parked — type-aware resolution for the cases syntax can't decide, opt-in). The restructure doesn't compete with the LSP work; it's the correct foundation under it. If you ever revive those branches, they'd slot in above the AST layer for precisely the residual cases the restructure still resolves heuristically — and the plan doc's "independent oracle" framing suggests that layering is what its author intended too.

For the current PR, the tree-sitter layer alone fixes every confirmed finding; nothing on the table needs the LSP tier.

input.description: Tree-sitter scanner restructure
input.prompt: You are doing a significant restructure in /Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph (Go repo, branch feat/retrieval-followups, working tree clean). Files you may edit: internal/sem/call_scanners.go, internal/sem/provider.go, internal/sem/parser.go, internal/sem/model.go, internal/sem/typescript_test.go (and you may add a new file like internal/sem/js_scopes.go). Do NOT commit, do NOT git add. Another agent is concurrently editing internal/sem/search_cache.go and internal/sem/search_cache_test.go only — do not touch those. ## Mission Replace the text-heuristic JS/TS scanning layer in internal/sem/call_scanners.go with scope data derived from the tree-sitter AST, eliminating an entire class of confirmed lexing bugs. The repo already parses every file with tree-sitter (see internal/sem/parser.go, ParseWithStatus and the grammar imports — javascript/typescript/tsx grammars are in use for symbol extraction). Two adversarial review rounds proved the regex/text approach unfixable: Round-2 confirmed bugs you must fix BY DESIGN (not by more heuristics): 1. jsRegexLiteralStartsAt treats '<' and '}' as regex-start contexts → JSX closing tags (``) start phantom regex masks that swallow JSX content and namespace calls in .tsx files. 2. stripJSScannedSource strips strings/comments BEFORE masking regexes → a quote inside a regex literal (`/'/g`) opens a phantom string, blanking real code including whole namespace declarations. 3. Regex after `)` or identifier is treated as division → `{` inside such regexes corrupts the brace-scope index (function scope extends to EOF, shadowing unrelated calls). 4. (provider.go ~3121) Statements inside a namespace body calling a nested namespace via relative receiver lose their CALLS edge: the top-level scan passes callerNamespace "" and jsCanonicalNamespace rejects receivers the old first-segment match accepted. Repro: `namespace A { export namespace B { export function f() {} } B.f(); }` — main emits a CALLS edge, branch emits none. Fix the resolution so statement-position calls inside namespace bodies resolve relative receivers. 5. for-of/for-in head destructuring (`for (const {X} of items)`) never registers a binding (jsAssignmentFollows requires '='). 6. jsTopLevelByteIndex/jsSplitParameterList count bare '<' comparisons as angle-bracket depth → later top-level commas missed, destructured bindings dropped (`const { a = x < 2, Utils } = opts`). 7. jsParameterNames finds the parameter list via first '(' → generic clauses with function types (`function run void>(Utils: T)`) extract wrong parameter names. Also fix by design (efficiency findings): the O(vars x symbols) var-binding scan in jsFileBindingScopes, and the 4x re-strip / 2x namespace-rescan per file (provider.go ~2587-2589 and ~2704 recompute derived state repeatedly). ## Design guidance (adapt as the code dictates) Build a single per-file scan state from one tree-sitter parse, e.g.: type jsScanState struct { namespaces []jsNamespaceScope; bindings []jsBindingScope; namespaceBySymbolID map[...]; parameterNamesBySymbol ...; braceScopes or AST-node scopes ... } computed by one function (parse once with the correct grammar for the file — reuse the language-selection logic parser.go already has, including tsx vs typescript vs javascript). From the AST you get exactly: namespace/module declarations and their body ranges and full paths; lexical_declaration/variable_declarator bindings including arbitrarily nested object/array destructuring patterns, for-of/for-in heads (statement scope = the loop node's range), catch clauses, function declarations and their scopes; formal_parameters (real parameter list node, no generic-clause confusion); var hoisting to the enclosing function node's range. Regex literals, template strings, JSX, comments are just node types — no masking needed anywhere. Integration notes: - Current consumers: provider.go relation building calls jsNamespaceScopes, jsNamespaceBySymbolID, jsFileBindingScopes, jsNamespaceCallIdentifiers/At, jsSymbolParameterNames, jsBindingShadowsAt, jsLexicalBindingShadowsAt, jsNamespaceMergesBinding etc. Map the call sites (git grep) and either keep thin wrappers over the new state or refactor call sites to build the state once per file and pass it through (preferred — that also fixes the 4x recompute). - Entities carry sourceStartByte/sourceEndByte (model.go) — useful for mapping symbols to AST scopes. - The call-identifier extraction itself (finding call expressions and receivers) may remain string-based where it is working, but its shadowing/scope decisions must come from the AST-derived state. If deriving call sites from the AST too is straightforward (call_expression nodes), prefer it — but do not expand scope beyond what the tests require; keep the change as small as a correct design allows. - Parse cost: prefer computing the scan state during/alongside the existing per-file parse path if the plumbing allows (parser.go ParseWithStatus already builds the tree); a second parse per JS/TS file during relation building is acceptable ONLY if wiring the existing parse through is genuinely invasive — if so, cache the state per file so it is computed at most once per snapshot build (fixes the 4x recompute either way). ## Regression tests Keep ALL existing tests passing unchanged — especially the five round-1 regression tests (TestTypeScriptCrossFileNamespaceMergeCallResolves, TestTypeScriptGenericReceiverKeepsInheritedMethodFallback, TestTypeScriptRegexLiteralBraceDoesNotCorruptBindingScopes, TestTypeScriptNestedDestructuringShadowsNamespaceCalls, TestTypeScriptLoopHeadBindingShadowConfinedToLoop) and all precision tests (shadowed names must not resolve; minified-bundle false positives must stay dead). Add new tests in typescript_test.go for each round-2 repro: - TSX component with `` closing tags and a namespace-qualified call inside JSX → edge present. - Quote and backtick inside regex literals before a namespace declaration → namespace calls still resolve. - Regex containing '{' after `)`/identifier context → no scope corruption, unrelated namespace call resolves. - `namespace A { export namespace B { export function f() {} } B.f(); }` → CALLS edge emitted (compare against main's behavior). - `for (const {Utils} of items) { Utils.helper(); }` with same-file namespace Utils → call shadowed, no phantom edge. - `const { a = x < 2, Utils } = opts;` → Utils registered as binding (shadowing works). - `function run void>(Utils: T)` → parameter Utils shadows namespace calls inside run. ## Verification gofmt -s on touched files (gofmt -l internal/sem clean for them), go vet ./internal/sem/, full go test -count=1 ./internal/sem/ (and ./internal/cli/ since CLI tests exercise search/neighbors paths). Also run a quick real-world smoke: go build -o /tmp/graph-check ./cmd/entire-graph is NOT needed; instead run go test -count=1 -run 'TypeScript|Javascript' ./internal/sem/ first for fast iteration, full suite at the end. Return raw facts: architecture of the new scan state (types, where built, how threaded), per-bug how the design eliminates it, tests added, dead code removed (the old regex helpers should be deleted, not stranded), final test results, and any behavior deltas you observed vs the heuristic version (cases where AST-derived answers differ).

input.description: Dedup selective-cache pipeline
input.prompt:
You are fixing a confirmed simplification finding in /Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph (Go repo, branch feat/retrieval-followups, working tree clean). Edit ONLY internal/sem/search_cache.go and internal/sem/search_cache_test.go (and internal/sem/search.go ONLY if the call site must change signature). Do NOT commit, do NOT git add. Another agent is concurrently editing call_scanners.go/provider.go/parser.go/model.go/typescript_test.go — do not touch those.

FINDING (search_cache.go ~172): loadOrDeriveSelectiveSearchSnapshot copy-pastes the key-compute/read/validate/derive/persist choreography of loadOrBuildSearchSnapshot (~lines 116-146) with only the full-snapshot source differing, and its failure fallback re-enters loadOrBuildSearchSnapshot, which re-resolves HEAD/repoKey, re-reads the selective cache entry it just found invalid, and re-reads the full snapshot from disk despite holding it in memory. Two ~30-line near-identical cache pipelines must be kept in sync; the derivation-failure path does double disk+subprocess work.

FIX: unify into one pipeline — e.g. give loadOrBuildSearchSnapshot an optional in-memory complete-snapshot parameter (or extract one shared helper both entry points call) so the warm path (preindexed full snapshot already in memory) and cold path share key computation, cache read/validate, derivation, persistence, and the soft-failure fallback, with HEAD/repoKey resolved once. Delete loadOrDeriveSelectiveSearchSnapshot if it becomes redundant. Preserve exact observable behavior: warm path consults the per-query selective cache first, persists on miss, falls back to fresh build on derivation error (regression tests TestWarmSelectiveSearchPersistsAndReusesPerQueryCache and TestWarmSelectiveDerivationFailureFallsBackToFreshBuild exist and must keep passing unchanged, as must TestPreindexProviderSnapshotServesSelectiveSearch and TestFullPreindexSearchMatchesColdSelectiveGraphExpansion).

Verify: gofmt on touched files, go vet ./internal/sem/, go test -count=1 -run 'Search|Cache|Preindex|Selective' ./internal/sem/, then full go test -count=1 ./internal/sem/. Return raw facts: the unified shape, what was deleted, test results.

content.text:
Both agents are underway:

1. **The restructure agent** is replacing the text-heuristic scanners with a per-file scan state derived from one tree-sitter parse — namespaces, bindings (including nested destructuring, for-of heads, catch clauses, var hoisting), and parameter names all read from AST nodes, where regex literals, JSX, and generics are just node types rather than lexing puzzles. Its brief requires all seven round-2 correctness bugs fixed *by design*, the namespace-body relative-receiver resolution repaired, both efficiency findings resolved (compute-once state), the old regex helpers deleted, all existing precision and round-1 regression tests kept green, and seven new regression tests mirroring the round-2 repros.
2. **The cache agent** is unifying the duplicated selective-snapshot pipeline so warm and cold paths share one key/read/validate/derive/persist flow, with the existing warm-path regression tests as the behavioral contract.

This is the largest change of the session, so the restructure agent will take a while. When both land: full verification, re-cut the four commits, the validation battery (bench, edge diffs including a `.tsx`-heavy check, cache equivalence), force-push to PR #45, and adversarial round 3 — which is the real test of whether the AST approach converges where the heuristics didn't.

**Unified shape** (all in /Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph/internal/sem/search_cache.go):

- `loadOrBuildSearchSnapshot` is now the single cache pipeline, with a new final parameter `preloadedFull *ProviderSnapshot`. One pass does: profile default → cache-bypass build (disableCache/no cacheDir/worktree) → `filepath.Abs` → `resolveCommittedHEAD` (build fallback on error) → `repoKey` → per-query key/read/validate (hit wins) → for `OnlyFiles` queries, a shared `deriveFromFull` closure that derives the selective view and best-effort persists it, tried first against `preloadedFull` (if non-nil, warm path — no disk read, no subprocess re-resolve) and then against the on-disk complete entry (cold path, and the warm derivation-failure fallback) → fresh build with the existing provenance check and best-effort persist. HEAD/repoKey/key/path are computed exactly once per call.
- `loadOrDeriveSelectiveSearchSnapshot` was reduced from a ~40-line duplicate pipeline to a one-line wrapper: `return loadOrBuildSearchSnapshot(ctx, repo, providerVersion, options, cacheDir, disableCache, &full)`. It was kept (not deleted) so the call site in search.go:358 and the direct call in `TestWarmSelectiveDerivationFailureFallsBackToFreshBuild` (search_cache_test.go:558) remain byte-for-byte unchanged.
- The three other internal callers (`loadOrBuildSearchGraphSnapshot`, `PreindexProviderSnapshot`, `LoadOrBuildProviderSnapshot`) pass `nil` for `preloadedFull`.

**What was deleted:** the duplicated key-compute/read/validate/derive/persist choreography inside `loadOrDeriveSelectiveSearchSnapshot`, including its re-entrant fallback that re-resolved HEAD/repoKey, re-read the just-invalidated selective entry, and re-read the full snapshot from disk. The derivation-failure path now falls through within the same call (disk-full-entry attempt at the freshly resolved HEAD, then fresh build), preserving the old fallback's observable behavior — in the HEAD-move scenario the stale disk entry fails validation at the new commit and a fresh build at the advanced HEAD is returned as a miss, which the regression test confirms.

**Not touched:** internal/sem/search.go, internal/sem/search_cache_test.go, and all files owned by the concurrent agent. Nothing staged or committed.

**Verification:**
- `gofmt -l` on search_cache.go, search_cache_test.go, search.go: clean.
- `go vet ./internal/sem/`: pass (only a cgo warning from the third-party go-tree-sitter/lua dependency).
- `go test -count=1 -run 'Search|Cache|Preindex|Selective' ./internal/sem/`: ok (12.1s) — includes TestWarmSelectiveSearchPersistsAndReusesPerQueryCache, TestWarmSelectiveDerivationFailureFallsBackToFreshBuild, TestPreindexProviderSnapshotServesSelectiveSearch, TestFullPreindexSearchMatchesColdSelectiveGraphExpansion.
- Full `go test -count=1 ./internal/sem/`: ok (35.5s).</result>

<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>Restructure complete. All suites green, working tree left uncommitted, search_cache files untouched.

## Architecture of the new scan state

**New file `/Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph/internal/sem/js_scopes.go` (712 lines).** Core type:

```go
type jsScanState struct {
    namespaces   []jsNamespaceScope  // body ranges + full dotted paths + declaring scope
    namespaceSet map[string]bool
    bindings     []jsBindingScope    // every shadow-capable binding with its byte-range scope
    calls        []jsDottedCall      // {parts []string, startByte, line}, sorted by startByte
    lineStarts   []int
    contentLen   int
    parsed       bool
}
```

Built by `newJSScanState(path, content)` → `jsScanGrammar` (mirrors ParseWithStatus routing: `.ts`→typescript grammar + `maskTypeScriptUnsupportedSyntax` (length-preserving), `.tsx`/`.jsx`→tsx, Flow-sniffed `.js`→tsx + flow mask, else javascript) → `buildJSScanState` does one parse and one recursive walk (`jsScopeWalker`) carrying `{lexStart/End, funcStart/End, namespace}` context. `newJSScanStateForContent` (typescript grammar, tsx fallback) backs the content-only test wrapper `jsNamespaceScopes`, whose signature — and `jsNamespaceBySymbolID`'s — is preserved because the untouchable `search_cache_test.go` calls both.

**Threading:** provider.go builds the state once per file (old lines 2583–2589), replacing `jsNamespaceScopes` + `jsNamespaceBySymbolID` + `jsFileBindingScopes` triple derivation. Consumers: `jsScan.namespaceCallsForSymbol(from)` (per-symbol, binary-searches sorted calls within the symbol's byte range, line-range fallback for byte-less symbols) and `jsScan.topLevelNamespaceCalls(currentFileSymbols)` (same line-coverage rule as `topLevelBlockFromLines`). Each call site is canonicalized at its own position: shadow check via `jsBindingShadowsAt(bindings, root, callByte)`, caller namespace via `jsNamespaceAtByte(namespaces, callByte)`. Parameter names now flow from the AST at parse time: `Entity.parameterNames` (model.go) populated in `walkEntitiesScoped` (parser.go) via `jsEntityParameterNames` (formal_parameters node), copied into `SymbolRecord.parameterNames` for the search-cache round-trip.

## Per-bug elimination

1. **`</td>` phantom regex** — JSX is `jsx_element`/`jsx_expression` nodes; no regex-start heuristic exists anymore. Calls inside JSX are ordinary `call_expression` nodes.
2. **Quote inside regex** — `regex` is a leaf node; no string/comment stripping happens before anything.
3. **`{` in regex after `)`/identifier** — no brace-scope index exists; scopes are `statement_block`/function node ranges.
4. **Namespace-body statement calls** — top-level calls no longer share one `callerNamespace ""`; each call canonicalizes through `jsNamespaceAtByte` at its position, so `B.f();` inside `namespace A` resolves to `A.B.f`.
5. **for-of/in destructured heads** — `for_in_statement` left-pattern bindings registered whenever a `var/let/const` token is present; no `=` requirement.
6. **`<` comparison as angle depth** — destructured names come from `object_pattern`/`object_assignment_pattern` nodes; `x < 2` is a `binary_expression` never entered.
7. **Generic clause with function type** — parameters come from the `parameters` field (`formal_parameters`), disjoint from `type_parameters`.
- **Efficiency:** var hoisting uses the walker's enclosing-function context (no O(vars×symbols) loop); one parse + one walk per file per snapshot build replaces 4× re-strip and 2× namespace rescans; per-symbol call lookup is O(log C).

## Tests

Added 7 (all in typescript_test.go): `TestTSXClosingTagsDoNotSwallowNamespaceCalls`, `TestTypeScriptQuoteAndBacktickInRegexKeepNamespaceCalls`, `TestTypeScriptRegexBraceInStatementPositionKeepsScopes`, `TestTypeScriptNamespaceBodyStatementCallResolvesRelativeReceiver`, `TestTypeScriptForOfDestructuredHeadShadowsNamespaceCall`, `TestTypeScriptDestructuringDefaultComparisonKeepsLaterBindings`, `TestTypeScriptGenericFunctionTypeClauseKeepsParameterShadow`. Replaced `TestJSParameterNamesStopsAtClosingListAndKeepsGenericCommas` (helper deleted) with `TestTypeScriptEntityParameterNamesComeFromFormalParameters` asserting the same guarantee from the AST path. Node-type assumptions were verified empirically against the vendored grammars (internal_module/module, nested_identifier, for_in_statement kind tokens, required/optional_parameter, switch_body, abstract_class_declaration) before writing the walker.

## Dead code removed

716 lines deleted from call_scanners.go: `stripJSScannedSource`, `maskJSRegexLiterals`, `jsRegexLiteralStartsAt/End`, `jsNamespaceCallIdentifiers(At)`, `jsFileBindingScopes`, `jsAssignmentFollows`, `jsForHeadBindingScope`, `jsNamespaceMergesBinding`, `jsSymbolParameterNames`, `jsLexicalBindingShadowsAt` (was already caller-less), `jsBraceScopes`+methods, `jsParameterNames`, `jsDestructuredNames`, `jsTopLevelByteIndex`, `jsSplitParameterList`, `jsDottedCallIdentifiers`, and 5 regexes. Kept-and-moved to js_scopes.go: `jsNamespaceScope`/`jsBindingScope` types, `jsBindingShadowsAt`, `jsCanonicalNamespace`, `jsNamespaceAtByte/Line`, `jsNamespaceBySymbolID` (its fallback now strips only strings/comments). Text-based `jsCallableArgumentIdentifiers` and `callLikeIdentifiers` remain, per scope guidance.

## Results

`gofmt -s -l internal/sem` clean; `go vet ./internal/sem/ ./internal/cli/` clean; `go test -count=1 ./...` fully green (sem 38s, cli 3.5s), including all five round-1 regression tests and the precision suite, unchanged.

## Behavior deltas vs the heuristic version

- Namespace calls inside template-string interpolations and JSX now resolve (old masking blanked them entirely).
- Optional-chained receivers (`A.B?.f()`) now flatten and resolve; the old regex could not match across `?.`.
- Block-less loop bodies (`for (const X of xs) X.f();`) are now correctly shadowed; loop-head scope is the whole loop statement node (old: brace block only, conservative fallback otherwise).
- `const K = class Name {}` no longer registers a phantom `Name` binding; `const enum E` binds `E` (old regex bound the literal name "enum").
- Files where tree-sitter fails outright (hard error/timeout) classify no namespace calls; partial parses with ERROR nodes still work since the walk tolerates error subtrees.
- `symbol.parameterNames` is now in declaration order (was sorted); the search-cache round-trip is order-agnostic.</result>

git add internal/sem/call_scanners.go internal/sem/js_scopes.go internal/sem/parser.go internal/sem/model.go internal/sem/provider.go internal/sem/typescript_test.go
git commit -q -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(sem): AST-based JS/TS call-scope resolution

Derive namespace scopes, lexical binding scopes (nested destructuring,
loop heads, catch clauses, var hoisting), dotted call sites, and
parameter names from a single tree-sitter parse per file instead of
regex/text scanning, so shadowed names never resolve to outer same-name
symbols and lexing hazards (regex literals, JSX, template strings,
generic clauses) cannot corrupt scope decisions by construction. Calls
canonicalize per call site, restoring namespace-body relative receivers
and cross-file namespace-merge fallback; receiver-type guards only
suppress inheritance fallbacks for types that resolve elsewhere. Also
stop attributing calls to symbols extracted from inline type-literal
properties. Eliminates large false-positive edge families in minified
bundles (lodash dist: -6.5k bogus "exact" CALLS edges) while adding
real resolutions (trpc: +337) in benchmark repos.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01AS8YV3giRoWagbRFo3ihAX
EOF
)"

git status --short; git log --oneline -5

input.command:
S=/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-thomi-Projects-entire-graph/fffff083-8032-496a-81ae-244c3c25ea0f/scratchpad
$S/bin/graph-wip edges --repo bench/.cache/JavaScript/lodash__lodash --format ndjson 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "
import json,sys
for line in sys.stdin:
    r=json.loads(line)
    if r.get('record_type')=='relation' and r.get('type')=='CALLS': print(r['from_id'],'->',r['to_id'])
" | sort > $S/lodash_ast.txt
echo "lodash CALLS: $(wc -l < $S/lodash_ast.txt | tr -d ' ')  removed-vs-main outside dist/: $(comm -23 <(awk '{print $1\" -> \"$3}' $S/lodash_calls_main.txt 2>/dev/null || cut -d' ' -f1-3 $S/lodash_calls_main.txt) $S/lodash_ast.txt 2>/dev/null | grep -vc ':dist/' || echo check)"
for q in "middleware chain handling" "context json rendering"; do
  $S/bin/graph-wip search --query "$q" --repo bench/.cache/Go/gin-gonic__gin --head --no-cache --format json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print('\n'.join(sorted(r['file_path']+':'+str(r.get('symbol_id','')) for r in d.get('results',[]))))" > $S/q3_cold.txt
  rm -rf $S/gc3 && mkdir -p $S/gc3 && $S/bin/graph-wip index --repo bench/.cache/Go/gin-gonic__gin --profile full --cache-dir $S/gc3 --head >/dev/null 2>&1
  $S/bin/graph-wip search --query "$q" --repo bench/.cache/Go/gin-gonic__gin --head --cache-dir $S/gc3 --format json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print('\n'.join(sorted(r['file_path']+':'+str(r.get('symbol_id','')) for r in d.get('results',[]))))" > $S/q3_warm.txt
  diff -q $S/q3_cold.txt $S/q3_warm.txt >/dev/null && echo "IDENTICAL: $q" || echo "DIFFER: $q"
done

input.name: code-review
input.args: high — round-3 adversarial review of PR #45's diff: four commits on feat/retrieval-followups vs main. Round 1 found 10 issues in the heuristic scanners (all fixed); round 2 found 7 new correctness bugs proving the regex/text-scanning approach unfixable, so the JS/TS scanning layer was RESTRUCTURED: internal/sem/js_scopes.go now derives namespace scopes, binding scopes (nested destructuring, for-of/for-in heads, catch clauses, var hoisting), dotted call sites, and parameter names from a single tree-sitter parse per file (jsScanState, built in provider.go relation setup); all regex/text scanning helpers were deleted; parameter names flow from formal_parameters AST nodes via Entity.parameterNames; the selective-search cache pipeline was unified into loadOrBuildSearchSnapshot with an optional preloaded in-memory snapshot. Attack the NEW code hardest: jsScopeWalker scope-context threading (lexStart/End, funcStart/End, namespace context), grammar routing in jsScanGrammar (ts vs tsx vs flow-sniffed js, maskTypeScriptUnsupportedSyntax length preservation), byte-range vs line-range symbol attribution in namespaceCallsForSymbol/topLevelNamespaceCalls, binding registration completeness (what binding forms does the walker miss — class declarations, import bindings, function expressions, labeled statements?), ERROR-node tolerance, the preloadedFull parameter's interaction with every loadOrBuildSearchSnapshot caller, and Entity.parameterNames serialization through the search cache. Also re-attack all prior angles for anything both rounds missed.

content.text:
The restructure is pushed to PR #45 (`7d1a8ad` at the tip, four clean commits) and round 3 of the adversarial review is running, briefed to attack the new AST code hardest: the scope-walker's context threading, grammar routing, byte-vs-line symbol attribution, binding-form completeness, ERROR-node tolerance, the unified cache pipeline's `preloadedFull` interactions, and parameter-name serialization — plus a re-sweep of every earlier angle.

Where things stand while it runs: all suites green, per-commit builds verified, bench relation sets identical to the validated heuristic version across all nine repos (the AST's behavior differences live precisely in the corner cases those repos don't contain), lodash precision intact, cache equivalence holds. The one honest cost: TypeScript indexing throughput is ~6% below `main` (11.0k vs 11.7k LOC/s) because the relation phase now runs a real parse — the price of scope decisions that can't be corrupted by lexing ambiguity. Round-3 verdict when the notification lands.

content.text: Round 3 shows real convergence — the findings dropped from 7 confirmed correctness regressions to **1**, with the rest being rare-declaration-shape corner cases and robustness gaps. Reading the truncated tail before acting:

input.description: Read round-3 findings 6-10

content.content:

F: A large or pathological TS/JS file parses within 5s during entity extraction but the second per-file parse in forEachRelation (provider.go:2586) times out under CPU load; every namespace-qualified CALLS edge for that file (previously found by the regex scan) silently disappears from `graph search`/`graph neighbors` output, nondeterministically betw 

--- 7. [PLAUSIBLE] correctness internal/sem/search_cache.go:171
S: The post-build provenance guard was widened from commit/tree (values genuinely versioned by git) to also require snapshot.Header.RepoKey == repositoryKey, but repoKey() is unstable: it reads `git remote` output and silently falls back to "local/<basename>" on any error, and the two evaluations (loadOrBuildSearchSnapshot's repositoryKey vs prepareSource's sc.key inside the build) run at different times.
F: User runs `entire graph search` (or `graph index` via PreindexProviderSnapshot, same new check at line ~239) while `git remote` transiently fails or while they add/remove the GitHub origin: one evaluation yields "gh/owner/repo" and the other "local/repo", so the command aborts with "repository identity or HEAD changed while building search snapshot 

--- 8. [CONFIRMED] correctness internal/cli/neighbors.go:663

F: An agent runs `graph neighbors --symbol Process --file wrong/path.go --format agent`; the (now normalized) file filter excludes every definition, output says 'No symbols matched "Process". Add --file to disambiguate a known definition.' The agent follows the hint, keeps or adds --file variants, and loops on zero results instead of being told to dro 

--- 9. [CONFIRMED] correctness internal/sem/search_cache.go:297
S: SymbolRecord.parameterNames is write-only plumbing: it is extracted from formal_parameters (parser.go:3565), copied to symbols (provider.go:1408), serialized as symbol_parameter_names and restored (search_cache.go:293-318), but a repo-wide trace finds zero consumers, so the unreliable signature-string parameter parsing it was documented to replace still drives all resolution. [same root cause also at: internal/sem/model.go:26]
F: The field comment (model.go:22) states signature parsing cannot recover parameter names when generic clauses contain parenthesized function types, yet every actual consumer of parameter names (types.go parameterNames(signature) at lines 1076/1120/1185/... feeding argumentForwardingFlows and other data-flow relations) still parses the signature text 

--- 10. [CONFIRMED] correctness internal/sem/search_cache.go:47

--- 10. [CONFIRMED] correctness internal/sem/search_cache.go:47
S: The deleted fast path in loadOrBuildSearchGraphSnapshot served the complete preindexed snapshot directly (old comment: full graph "gives relation expansion repository-wide context" and "performs no query-time relation re-resolution"); the replacement always derives an OnlyFiles-confined graph, and nothing re-establishes repository-wide expansion or the direct-load cost profile. findings.summary: Byte-exact per-symbol scan plus line-granular top-level scan creates a coverage crack: any call statement sharing a source line with a declaration loses its CALLS edge entirely (regression vs main, verified by differential probe).
findings.failure_scenario: TS file with `export function helper() {}` and `export function f() {} helper();` on one line: the top-level scan excludes the line (covered by symbol f, line granularity) while the per-symbol scan uses f's exact byte range, which excludes the trailing `helper();`. Main emitted CALLS f->helper; the branch emits nothing, so `graph neighbors --symbol helper` reports zero callers. Affects compact/minified/multi-statement-per-line JS/TS broadly.

findings.summary: Merged class+namespace declarations route static-method calls into the bare terminal-name fallback, which can fabricate a cross-file CALLS edge to an unrelated same-named function.
findings.failure_scenario: `class A { static create() {} }` merged with `namespace A { export const version = 1 }`; a same-file `A.create()` call is classified as a namespace call, finds no namespace member (the static method's namespace is ""), and falls back to resolving bare `create` workspace-wide — a phantom edge to any other file's `create`, or the true static-method edge dropped.

findings.summary: Binding shadow check ignores lexical nesting priority: an outer-scope function/class binding suppresses namespace classification even where an inner same-name namespace is the correct resolution.
findings.failure_scenario: `function A() {} namespace B { namespace A { export function f() {} } export function g() { A.f(); } }` — the top-level `function A` binding spans the whole file and shadows the `A.f()` call inside B.g, although TypeScript resolves `A` there to the nearer `B.A`. The CALLS edge from B.g to B.A.f is silently lost.

findings.summary: Named function-expression and class-expression self-name bindings are never registered, so their names cannot shadow a same-file namespace inside their own bodies.
findings.failure_scenario: `namespace Utils { export function helper() {} } const x = (function Utils() { Utils.helper(); })();` — inside the named function expression, `Utils` binds to the function itself, but the walker registers only declaration self-names; the call is misclassified as a namespace call and a phantom CALLS edge to the namespace member is emitted.

findings.summary: jsQualifiedNamespaceName treats a nested dotted declaration whose name starts with the parent path as already fully qualified, but TypeScript resolves `namespace A.B` inside `namespace A` relative to the parent (declaring A.A.B).
findings.failure_scenario: `namespace A { namespace A.B { export function f() {} } }` declares A.A.B in TypeScript, but the HasPrefix branch records the scope as "A.B". Top-level `A.B.f()` gains a phantom same-file resolution while a correctly-scoped `B.f()` inside namespace A resolves through the wrong canonical name.

findings.summary: buildJSScanState silently returns an empty scan state when the relation-phase tree-sitter reparse errors or exceeds the 5s timeout, recording no PartialFailure — unlike the entity-parse timeout path.
findings.failure_scenario: A large file parses within 5s during entity extraction but the second relation-phase parse times out under CPU load: every namespace-qualified CALLS edge for that file silently disappears, nondeterministically between runs, with no diagnostic in the snapshot's partial-failure report.

findings.summary: The post-build provenance guard now also requires Header.RepoKey == repositoryKey, but repoKey() is unstable (git remote output with a silent local/<basename> fallback) and the two evaluations run at different times.
findings.failure_scenario: `graph search` or `graph index` runs while `git remote` transiently fails or the origin is being added/removed: one evaluation yields gh/owner/repo and the other local/repo, and the command aborts with 'repository identity or HEAD changed while building search snapshot' for a repo whose git state is fine.

findings.summary: The zero-match agent message still says 'Add --file to disambiguate a known definition', which contradicts the PR's ID-based guidance and can never produce a match (adding --file only narrows further).
findings.failure_scenario: An agent gets zero matches because its --file filter excluded every definition, and the hint tells it to add --file — the agent loops on zero results instead of being told to drop --file or use the symbol ID.

findings.summary: SymbolRecord.parameterNames is write-only plumbing: extracted from formal_parameters, serialized and restored through the search cache, but zero consumers — the unreliable signature-string parsing it was documented to replace still drives all resolution.
findings.failure_scenario: The field comment states signature parsing cannot recover parameter names for generic clauses with parenthesized function types, yet types.go's parameterNames(signature) still feeds argumentForwardingFlows and other data-flow relations. Either wire the AST-derived names into those consumers or drop the dead serialization.

findings.summary: The deleted fast path served the complete preindexed snapshot directly for repository-wide relation expansion; the replacement always derives an OnlyFiles-confined graph. Flagged as regression, but confinement is this PR's documented intent — needs an explicit design confirmation rather than code change.
findings.failure_scenario: On a warm preindex, search output externalizes relation-expansion targets in files outside the query-selected set that the previous release resolved repository-wide. The README and commit message document confinement as the deliberate new contract (warm == cold results); if that contract is confirmed, this finding is working-as-intended and only the cost profile (addressed by per-query persistence) remains.
