input.description: Fix round-3 sem findings
input.prompt:
You are fixing verified round-3 code-review findings in /Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph (Go repo, branch feat/retrieval-followups, working tree has one unrelated pending edit in internal/cli/neighbors.go — leave it alone). Files you may edit: internal/sem/js_scopes.go, internal/sem/provider.go, internal/sem/parser.go, internal/sem/model.go, internal/sem/types.go, internal/sem/search_cache.go, internal/sem/typescript_test.go, internal/sem/search_cache_test.go. Do NOT commit or git add.

Context: internal/sem/js_scopes.go derives JS/TS scope state (jsScanState) from one tree-sitter parse; provider.go consumes it in forEachRelation (~2583-2620) via namespaceCallsForSymbol/topLevelNamespaceCalls. Fix these findings without regressing any existing test (all suites currently green):

FINDING 1 (MOST IMPORTANT — provider.go ~2604, js_scopes.go ~233): Byte-exact per-symbol scan + line-granular top-level scan leaves a coverage crack. Repro (verified): one line containing `export function f() {} helper();` — top-level scan excludes the whole line (line covered by symbol f), per-symbol scan uses f's exact byte range which excludes the trailing `helper();` — the CALLS edge for helper() vanishes (main emitted it). Fix by making coverage exact and complementary: e.g. make topLevelNamespaceCalls/the top-level general scan operate on byte ranges too — a call belongs to a symbol iff its byte offset lies within that symbol's source byte range; everything else is top-level. Symbols lacking byte ranges (non-JS languages or synthesized entities) keep the line fallback, but for files where byte ranges exist the partition must be exact (no call site attributed to nobody, none to two owners). Add a regression test with a declaration and a call sharing one line (both directions: call after decl on the same line, and a decl after a call).

FINDING 2 (provider.go ~2793): Merged class+namespace: `class A { static create() {} }` + `namespace A { export const version = 1 }` — a same-file `A.create()` call classifies as a namespace call, finds no namespace member, and the matched=false fallback resolves bare `create` workspace-wide (phantom cross-file edge). Fix: when the receiver root also names a same-file class/enum/object symbol (declaration merging), the fallback must first try member resolution on that merged symbol (e.g. resolve A.create against class A's methods via the existing receiver/member resolution paths) and only then consider generic fallback — and never resolve the bare terminal name workspace-wide when the receiver names a same-file merged declaration. Test: static method call resolves to the class's static method, no phantom edge to an unrelated same-named function in another file.

FINDING 3 (js_scopes.go ~189): Shadow check ignores lexical nesting priority. Repro: `function A() {} namespace B { namespace A { export function f() {} } export function g() { A.f(); } }` — the file-wide `function A` binding shadows the call although the inner namespace B.A is nearer. Fix: when both a binding and a namespace scope contain the call site, the innermost (smallest enclosing scope) declaration wins — i.e. shadow only if the binding's scope is nested inside (or equal to) the nearest enclosing scope in which a matching namespace is visible; concretely, compare the binding scope's start against the containing namespace scope's range and prefer the deeper one. Test the repro (edge B.g -> B.A.f must exist) plus a control where the binding is genuinely inner and must still shadow.

FINDING 4 (js_scopes.go ~305): Named function-expression / class-expression self-names are not registered as bindings scoped to their own bodies. Repro: `const x = (function Utils() { Utils.helper(); })();` with a same-file `namespace Utils` — phantom edge. Fix: in the walker, when visiting `function_expression`/`class` (expression form) nodes with a name child, register a binding for the name whose scope is the expression node's own range. Test the repro.

FINDING 5 (js_scopes.go ~377): `namespace A { namespace A.B {...} }` — jsQualifiedNamespaceName's HasPrefix(name, parent+".") branch records "A.B" but TypeScript declares A.A.B (dotted names are parent-relative). Fix: always qualify dotted declaration names against the parent path (drop the HasPrefix special case) unless there is a documented reason it exists — check git log/comments first; if the special case guards something real, handle both. Test: nested `namespace A { namespace A.B { export function f() {} B... } }` shapes resolve to A.A.B, and a top-level `A.B.f()` call does NOT resolve to it.

FINDING 6 (js_scopes.go ~119): buildJSScanState silently returns an empty scan state on relation-phase parse error/timeout, with no PartialFailure recorded (entity-phase timeouts do record one — see parser.go's handling). Fix: surface the failure — thread an error/status from newJSScanState to the provider call site and record a PartialFailure (matching the existing partial-failure plumbing and codes) so a timed-out relation parse is observable. Keep behavior otherwise (no namespace calls classified for that file).

FINDING 7 (search_cache.go ~171 and ~239): The post-build provenance guard compares Header.RepoKey against a repoKey() computed at a different time; repoKey reads `git remote` and silently falls back to local/<basename>, so transient remote failures abort with a spurious 'repository identity or HEAD changed while building search snapshot'. Fix: drop RepoKey from the provenance comparison (commit/tree are the genuinely versioned values) OR resolve the key once and pass it into the build so both sides use the same value — choose whichever matches the surrounding design better; add a comment stating why. Adjust/extend tests accordingly.

FINDING 9 (search_cache.go ~297, model.go ~26, types.go): SymbolRecord.parameterNames is write-only — extracted from the AST, serialized through the search cache, restored, never read. The documented purpose was replacing signature-string parsing (types.go parameterNames(signature) callers ~1076/1120/1185 feeding argumentForwardingFlows etc.). Fix properly: wire the AST-derived names into those consumers — prefer symbol.parameterNames when non-empty, falling back to signature parsing for symbols without AST data (other languages). Add a test showing a TS signature with a parenthesized function type in a generic clause now yields correct data-flow parameter names (the case signature parsing gets wrong). If wiring in reveals the signature parser is used for languages the AST path doesn't cover, keep the fallback — do not delete the parser.

Do NOT change the OnlyFiles confinement design (round-3 finding 10 was working-as-intended).

Verification: gofmt -s (gofmt -l internal/sem clean for touched files), go vet ./internal/sem/, go test -count=1 ./internal/sem/ full, plus ./internal/cli/ (neighbors tests must stay green — do not touch cli files). Return raw facts: per-finding fix summary, tests added, results, and any finding where you had to deviate from the suggested approach and why.

Repro: `const x = (function Utils() { Utils.helper(); })();` with a same-file `namespace Utils` — phantom edge. Fix: in the walker, when visiting `function_expression`/`class` (expression form) nodes with a name child, register a binding for the name whose scope is the expression node's own range. Test the repro.

FINDING 5 (js_scopes.go ~377): `namespace A { namespace A.B {...} }` — jsQualifiedNamespaceName's HasPrefix(name, parent+".") branch records "A.B" but TypeScript declares A.A.B (dotted names are parent-relative). Fix: always qualify dotted declaration names against the parent path (drop the HasPrefix special case) unless there is a documented reason it exists — check git log/comments first; if the special case guards something real, handle both. Test: nested `namespace A { namespace A.B { export function f() {} B... } }` shapes resolve to A.A.B, and a top-level `A.B.f()` call does NOT resolve to it.

FINDING 6 (js_scopes.go ~119): buildJSScanState silently returns an empty scan state on relation-phase parse error/timeout, with no PartialFailure recorded (entity-phase timeouts do record one — see parser.go's handling). Fix: surface the failure — thread an error/status from newJSScanState to the provider call site and record a PartialFailure (matching the existing partial-failure plumbing and codes) so a timed-out relation parse is observable. Keep behavior otherwise (no namespace calls classified for that file).

FINDING 7 (search_cache.go ~171 and ~239): The post-build provenance guard compares Header.RepoKey against a repoKey() computed at a different time; repoKey reads `git remote` and silently falls back to local/<basename>, so transient remote failures abort with a spurious 'repository identity or HEAD changed while building search snapshot'. Fix: drop RepoKey from the provenance comparison (commit/tree are the genuinely versioned values) OR resolve the key once and pass it into the build so both sides use the same value — choose whichever matches the surrounding design better; add a comment stating why. Adjust/extend tests accordingly.

FINDING 9 (search_cache.go ~297, model.go ~26, types.go): SymbolRecord.parameterNames is write-only — extracted from the AST, serialized through the search cache, restored, never read. The documented purpose was replacing signature-string parsing (types.go parameterNames(signature) callers ~1076/1120/1185 feeding argumentForwardingFlows etc.). Fix properly: wire the AST-derived names into those consumers — prefer symbol.parameterNames when non-empty, falling back to signature parsing for symbols without AST data (other languages). Add a test showing a TS signature with a parenthesized function type in a generic clause now yields correct data-flow parameter names (the case signature parsing gets wrong). If wiring in reveals the signature parser is used for languages the AST path doesn't cover, keep the fallback — do not delete the parser.

Do NOT change the OnlyFiles confinement design (round-3 finding 10 was working-as-intended).

Verification: gofmt -s (gofmt -l internal/sem clean for touched files), go vet ./internal/sem/, go test -count=1 ./internal/sem/ full, plus ./internal/cli/ (neighbors tests must stay green — do not touch cli files). Return raw facts: per-finding fix summary, tests added, results, and any finding where you had to deviate from the suggested approach and why.

content.text:
Round-3 remediation is underway. I fixed the misleading zero-match hint in `neighbors.go` directly (it now tells agents to *drop* `--file` or use the symbol ID, instead of the self-defeating "add `--file`"), and a single sem-side agent is fixing the rest: the scan-gap regression via exact byte-range partitioning (the one must-fix), the four namespace corner cases (merged-declaration member resolution, innermost-scope-wins shadowing, expression self-names, parent-relative dotted namespace names), the silent-reparse-timeout observability gap, the unstable repoKey provenance guard, and wiring the AST-derived parameter names into their intended consumers so the plumbing stops being write-only. Finding 10 needs no code change — the confinement behavior it flagged is the PR's documented contract.

After the agent lands: full verification, re-cut, validation spot-checks, push, and a final review round to confirm convergence.

<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>All findings fixed, all suites green. Final report:

## Per-finding summary

**Finding 1 (coverage crack, byte-exact partition)** — `js_scopes.go`, `provider.go`. `topLevelNamespaceCalls` now partitions dotted calls by `symbolByteRange` (exact tree-sitter bytes, whole-line fallback only for symbols without byte metadata) instead of line coverage. Added `jsScanState.topLevelBlock`, which blanks each symbol's exact byte range with spaces (preserving offsets/lines); the JS/TS top-level general scan (`provider.go` ~3095) uses it instead of line-granular `topLevelBlockFromLines`, so the residual text is the exact complement of the per-symbol blocks (`exactSymbolSource`). Other languages keep the line-based mask. Test: `TestTypeScriptSameLineDeclarationAndCallKeepsTopLevelCallEdge` — both directions (call after decl, decl after call), asserting the file→helper edge exists and the call is not attributed to the same-line symbol.

**Finding 2 (merged class+namespace phantom fallback)** — `provider.go`. New `resolveJSMergedDeclarationMemberTargets` runs in both namespace-call fallback sites (per-symbol ~2795, top-level ~3245) before the generic terminal-name fallback: when the receiver path also names a same-file class/enum/object (qualified via `jsSymbolNamespaces`), it resolves the terminal against that declaration's members (`ContainerID` match); if the receiver names such a merged declaration, generic workspace-wide fallback is suppressed entirely. Test: `TestTypeScriptMergedClassNamespaceStaticCallResolvesToClassMethod` — `A.create()` resolves to class A's static method in-file, no edge to same-named `create` in another file.

**Finding 3 (lexical nesting vs shadow)** — `js_scopes.go`. `qualifiedNamespaceCall` now canonicalizes first (via new `jsCanonicalNamespaceWithPrefix`, which also returns the enclosing-namespace prefix the match resolved through), then `bindingShadowsNamespaceReceiver` anchors visibility at the innermost enclosing namespace scope named that prefix (whole file when top-level): a binding shadows only if its scope starts at/inside that anchor. Test: `TestTypeScriptNearerNamespaceBeatsOuterBindingShadow` — `B.g -> B.A.f` exists despite file-wide `function A`; control with a genuinely inner parameter binding still shadows.

**Finding 4 (named expression self-names)** — `js_scopes.go` walker: `function`/`function_expression`/`generator_function` and class-expression (`class`) nodes with a name child register a binding scoped to the expression node (`addExpressionSelfNameBinding`). Test: `TestTypeScriptNamedExpressionSelfNameShadowsNamespace` (IIFE and class-expression variants, plus unshadowed control).

**Finding 5 (dotted nested namespace names)** — `js_scopes.go`. `jsQualifiedNamespaceName` always qualifies against the parent (dropped both the `HasPrefix` and `name == parent` special cases — git history shows they date from the file's initial commit with no guarded behavior beyond the incorrect "already carries the full path" assumption; TS dotted declarations are parent-relative). Test: `TestTypeScriptNestedDottedNamespaceQualifiesAgainstParent` — calls inside/beside `namespace A { namespace A.B }` resolve to A.A.B; top-level `A.B.f()` does not.

**Finding 6 (silent relation-phase parse failure)** — `js_scopes.go`, `provider.go`, `search_cache.go`. `newJSScanState`/`buildJSScanState` now return an error (timeout wraps `context.DeadlineExceeded`; timeout budget moved to var `jsScanParseTimeout` for deterministic testing). `forEachRelation` gained a `recordFailure func(PartialFailure)` parameter; the provider maps the error via `jsScanPartialFailure` (E_PARSE_TIMEOUT / E_PARSE_ERROR, matching entity-phase codes) into the summary's `failures`; the selective-derivation path in `search_cache.go` merges recorded failures with the filtered full-build failures (`mergePartialFailures`, deduped by code+file). Behavior otherwise unchanged (empty scan state, no namespace calls). Test: `TestTypeScriptRelationScanParseTimeoutRecordsPartialFailure` (1ns budget on a 2000-function file; asserts E_PARSE_TIMEOUT with the relation-phase effect text and intact entity-phase symbols; verified stable across 5 runs).

**Finding 7 (spurious RepoKey provenance abort)** — `search_cache.go`. Both post-build guards (`loadOrBuildSearchSnapshot`, `PreindexProviderSnapshot`) now use `searchSnapshotProvenanceChanged`, which compares only commit/tree; the doc comment explains RepoKey is read-time-derived from `git remote` with a silent local fallback, and that identity changes are still honored by `validCachedSearchSnapshot` on reads (the existing `TestSearchSnapshotCacheDoesNotReuseRepoKeyAfterRemoteChanges` still passes). Tests: `TestSearchSnapshotProvenanceIgnoresRepoKeyDrift`, `TestMergePartialFailuresDeduplicatesByCodeAndFile`.

**Finding 9 (write-only parameterNames)** — `types.go`, `provider.go`. All nine forwarding-flow helpers (`argumentForwardingFlows` … `directLiteralForwardingFlows`) now take a `params map[string]bool` computed once by `returnFlowCalls`; the provider passes `symbolFlowParameterNames(from)`, which prefers AST-derived `symbol.parameterNames` and falls back to `parameterNames(signature)` for symbols without AST data (parser kept, per instruction). Test: `TestTypeScriptASTParameterNamesDriveDataFlowForwarding` — `run<T extends (helper: string) => void>(input: string)` yields the `input -> target` DATA_FLOWS edge and no phantom flow from the generic-clause identifier.

## Deviations from suggested approaches
- Finding 6's test forces the timeout via a package var (`jsScanParseTimeout`) plus a large file rather than the const budget — smacker's `ParseCtx` cancellation is flag-based and a tiny file finishes before the watcher goroutine fires.
- Finding 7: chose "drop RepoKey from the comparison" (extracted into a commented, unit-testable helper) rather than threading a pre-resolved key into the build, since the build API (`prepareSource`) has no key-injection seam and commit/tree fully pin provenance.

## Verification
- `gofmt -l internal/sem` clean; `go vet ./internal/sem/` clean.
- `go test -count=1 ./internal/sem/` ok (36s, includes 9 new tests/subtests); `./internal/cli/` ok (neighbors untouched); full `go test ./...` ok.
- `internal/cli/neighbors.go` pending edit left as-is; nothing committed or staged. `model.go`/`parser.go` needed no changes (existing plumbing already carried `parameterNames`).</result>

33		// FileRecord.Lines, SymbolRecord.Local, and exact symbol byte ranges are
34		// intentionally absent from the public wire format, but relation resolution
35		// consumes them. Preserve those internal fields so a complete preindex can
36		// derive an exact selective view without reparsing source files.
37		FileLines            map[string]int                   `json:"file_lines,omitempty"`

41	}
42	
43	// loadOrBuildSearchGraphSnapshot preserves the exact candidate-file scope even
44	// when a complete committed-tree snapshot is published concurrently. The
45	// shared loader derives an OnlyFiles view from that full snapshot and reports a
46	// cache hit, so cache timing cannot change the graph search receives.
47	func loadOrBuildSearchGraphSnapshot(
48		ctx context.Context,
49		repo, providerVersion string,
50		options ProviderSnapshotOptions,
51		cacheDir string,
52		disableCache bool,
53	) (ProviderSnapshot, bool, error) {
54		return loadOrBuildSearchSnapshot(ctx, repo, providerVersion, options, cacheDir, disableCache, nil)
55	}
56	
57	func loadCachedCompleteSearchSnapshot(
58		ctx context.Context,
59		repo, providerVersion string,
60		options ProviderSnapshotOptions,
61		cacheDir string,
62	) (ProviderSnapshot, bool, error) {
63		if cacheDir == "" || options.Worktree {
64			return ProviderSnapshot{}, false, nil
65		}
66		absRepo, err := filepath.Abs(repo)
67		if err != nil {
68			return ProviderSnapshot{}, false, err
69		}
70		commit, tree, headErr := resolveCommittedHEAD(ctx, absRepo)
71		if headErr != nil {
72			return ProviderSnapshot{}, false, nil
73		}
74		repositoryKey := repoKey(ctx, absRepo)
75		fullOptions := options
76		fullOptions.OnlyFiles = nil
77		if fullOptions.Profile == "" {
78			fullOptions.Profile = ProfileFull
79		}
80		fullKey, keyErr := searchSnapshotKey(absRepo, repositoryKey, providerVersion, commit, tree, fullOptions)
81		if keyErr != nil {
82			return ProviderSnapshot{}, false, keyErr
83		}
84		fullPath := filepath.Join(cacheDir, "search", searchSnapshotCacheVersion, fullKey+".json.gz")
85		cached, readErr := readSearchSnapshot(fullPath)
86		if readErr != nil || !validCachedSearchSnapshot(cached, repositoryKey, providerVersion, commit, tree, fullOptions) {
87			return ProviderSnapshot{}, false, nil
88		}
89		return cached.Snapshot, true, nil
90	}
91	
92	// loadOrBuildSearchSnapshot is the single search-snapshot cache pipeline: it
93	// resolves HEAD and the repository key once, serves a valid per-query cache
94	// entry first, otherwise derives a selective view from a complete
95	// committed-tree snapshot (the optional preloadedFull already in memory, then
96	// the on-disk complete entry) and persists it, and finally falls back to a
97	// fresh build. Derivation failures are soft so an optional cache can never
98	// break retrieval.
99	func loadOrBuildSearchSnapshot(
100		ctx context.Context,
101		repo, providerVersion string,
102		options ProviderSnapshotOptions,
103		cacheDir string,
104		disableCache bool,
105		preloadedFull *ProviderSnapshot,
106	) (ProviderSnapshot, bool, error) {
107		if options.Profile == "" {
108			options.Profile = ProfileFull
109		}
110		if disableCache || cacheDir == "" || options.Worktree {
111			snapshot, err := BuildProviderSnapshotWithOptions(ctx, repo, providerVersion, options)
112			return snapshot, false, err
113		}
114		absRepo, err := filepath.Abs(repo)
115		if err != nil {
116			return ProviderSnapshot{}, false, err
117		}
118		commit, tree, headErr := resolveCommittedHEAD(ctx, absRepo)
119		if headErr != nil {
120			snapshot, buildErr := BuildProviderSnapshotWithOptions(ctx, repo, providerVersion, options)
121			return snapshot, false, buildErr
122		}
123		repositoryKey := repoKey(ctx, absRepo)
124		key, err := searchSnapshotKey(absRepo, repositoryKey, providerVersion, commit, tree, options)
125		if err != nil {
126			return ProviderSnapshot{}, false, err
127		}
128		path := filepath.Join(cacheDir, "search", searchSnapshotCacheVersion, key+".json.gz")
129		if cached, err := readSearchSnapshot(path); err == nil && validCachedSearchSnapshot(cached, repositoryKey, providerVersion, commit, tree, options) {
130			return cached.Snapshot, true, nil
131		}
132		// A complete committed-tree snapshot is query independent and can serve a
133		// selective search without rebuilding the same tree for every query. Keep
134		// the selective view so cache presence cannot change retrieval semantics.
135		if len(options.OnlyFiles) > 0 {
136			deriveFromFull := func(full ProviderSnapshot) (ProviderSnapshot, bool) {
137				selective, deriveErr := selectiveSearchSnapshotFromFull(ctx, absRepo, providerVersion, options, full)
138				if deriveErr != nil {
139					// Provenance or internal-metadata mismatches make this complete
140					// snapshot unsuitable for derivation. Fall through instead of
141					// letting an optional cache break retrieval.
142					return ProviderSnapshot{}, false
143				}
144				// Persisting the exact selective view makes repeated identical queries
145				// a direct cache hit. As with ordinary search caching, this is best effort.
146				_ = writeSearchSnapshot(path, newCachedSearchSnapshot(providerVersion, commit, tree, options, selective))
147				return selective, true
148			}
149			if preloadedFull != nil {
150				if selective, ok := deriveFromFull(*preloadedFull); ok {
151					return selective, true, nil
152				}
153			}
154			fullOptions := options
155			fullOptions.OnlyFiles = nil
156			fullKey, keyErr := searchSnapshotKey(absRepo, repositoryKey, providerVersion, commit, tree, fullOptions)
157			if keyErr != nil {
158				return ProviderSnapshot{}, false, keyErr
159			}
160			fullPath := filepath.Join(cacheDir, "search", searchSnapshotCacheVersion, fullKey+".json.gz")
161			if cached, readErr := readSearchSnapshot(fullPath); readErr == nil && validCachedSearchSnapshot(cached, repositoryKey, providerVersion, commit, tree, fullOptions) {
162				if selective, ok := deriveFromFull(cached.Snapshot); ok {
163					return selective, true, nil
164				}
165			}
166		}
167		snapshot, err := BuildProviderSnapshotWithOptions(ctx, repo, providerVersion, options)
168		if err != nil {
169			return ProviderSnapshot{}, false, err
170		}
171		if searchSnapshotProvenanceChanged(snapshot.Header, commit, tree) {
172			return ProviderSnapshot{}, false, fmt.Errorf(
173				"HEAD changed while building search snapshot: got commit %q tree %q, started at commit %q tree %q",
174				snapshot.Header.Commit, snapshot.Header.Tree, commit, tree,
175			)
176		}
177		cache := newCachedSearchSnapshot(providerVersion, commit, tree, options, snapshot)
178		// Cache persistence is best effort. Retrieval correctness never depends on
179		// a writable cache directory.
180		_ = writeSearchSnapshot(path, cache)
181		return snapshot, false, nil
182	}

input.name: code-review
input.args: high — round-4 (final gate) adversarial review of PR #45: four commits on feat/retrieval-followups vs main. History: round 1 found 10 heuristic-scanner bugs (fixed), round 2 found 7 more proving regex scanning unfixable (led to full tree-sitter restructure in internal/sem/js_scopes.go), round 3 found 1 confirmed regression + 4 namespace corner cases + robustness gaps against the AST code — all now fixed: byte-exact call partitioning (jsScanState.topLevelBlock blanking exact symbol ranges, symbolByteRange attribution with line fallback), merged class+namespace member resolution before fallback (resolveJSMergedDeclarationMemberTargets, suppressed workspace fallback for merged receivers), innermost-scope-wins shadowing (jsCanonicalNamespaceWithPrefix + bindingShadowsNamespaceReceiver anchoring), named function/class-expression self-name bindings, parent-relative dotted namespace qualification, relation-phase parse failures recorded as PartialFailures (forEachRelation recordFailure param, mergePartialFailures), provenance guard narrowed to commit/tree only (searchSnapshotProvenanceChanged), AST parameterNames wired into all nine forwarding-flow helpers with signature-parsing fallback, and the neighbors zero-match hint rewritten. Attack the round-3 fixes hardest: the byte-exact partition (overlapping symbol ranges? nested symbols? symbols with ranges in some files and not others? blanking correctness), the shadow-anchoring logic, merged-declaration member resolution (does suppressing workspace fallback lose legitimate edges?), the recordFailure threading through all forEachRelation callers, and the parameterNames map threading. Re-attack all prior angles. This is the final gate: report only findings that genuinely survive adversarial verification.

content.text:
Round 4 — the final gate — is running, and CI on the pushed branch is nearly green already (6 of 7 passed, only the slow Windows test job still pending). The current state of PR #45:

- Four clean commits, each building independently (including the intermediate `forEachRelation` call-site shim that keeps the scanner commit self-contained).
- All 27 findings across three adversarial rounds addressed: 10 heuristic fixes, then the tree-sitter restructure that eliminated the heuristic bug class outright, then 9 targeted repairs to the AST code.
- Final validation: every real-code bench repo net-positive vs `main` (nest +52, trpc +432, axios +42, vite +22, frp +42), lodash's junk elimination at −11,455 with zero removals outside minified bundles and 17 restored real-source edges, parse failures unchanged, cache cold/warm equivalence intact.

If round 4 comes back clean — which the round-over-round trajectory (10 correctness → 7 → 1 → ?) suggests it should — the PR is ready to merge with a merge commit. I'll report the verdict when it lands.

input.description: Read round-4 tail findings

content.content:
10 findings
--- 5. [PLAUSIBLE] correctness internal/sem/provider.go:2671
S: The deleted regex scanner jsNamespaceCallIdentifiers worked on any file content with no parse budget; its replacement requires a second full tree-sitter parse per JS/TS file (newJSScanState with jsScanParseTimeout), and on parse failure/timeout the file's namespace-qualified call
--- 6. [CONFIRMED] cleanup internal/sem/provider.go:3264
S: The three-step namespace-call resolution fallback chain (resolveJSNamespaceCallTargets -> resolveJSMergedDeclarationMemberTargets -> terminal-name resolveCallTargets) is copy-pasted in the per-symbol path (~line 2869) and the top-level path (~line 3264) with one subtle guard diff
--- 7. [CONFIRMED] cleanup internal/sem/provider.go:2115
S: forEachRelation's signature has grown to nine positional parameters (two of them nilable callbacks) with every cross-cutting concern added as another argument threaded through all callers
--- 8. [CONFIRMED] cleanup internal/sem/js_scopes.go:278
S: qualifiedNamespaceCall is recomputed per covering symbol for the same call site (nested symbol ranges like class + its methods overlap), and topLevelNamespaceCalls does an O(symbols) linear span scan per call instead of classifying each call once
--- 9. [CONFIRMED] cleanup internal/sem/parser.go:6420
S: javascriptVariableDeclaratorEnd calls stripCodeLiteralsAndComments on the entire file content once per exported-variable match instead of the caller stripping once
--- 10. [CONFIRMED] cleanup internal/sem/provider.go:2673
S: jsNamespaceBySymbolID runs unconditionally for every scanned JS/TS file, paying a full-file stripCodeLiteralsAndComments pass (plus per-symbol regex fallback compiles) even when the scan found zero namespaces, which is the common case

toolUseResult.stdout:
10 findings
--- 5. [PLAUSIBLE] correctness internal/sem/provider.go:2671
S: The deleted regex scanner jsNamespaceCallIdentifiers worked on any file content with no parse budget; its replacement requires a second full tree-sitter parse per JS/TS file (newJSScanState with jsScanParseTimeout), and on parse failure/timeout the file's namespace-qualified call
--- 6. [CONFIRMED] cleanup internal/sem/provider.go:3264
S: The three-step namespace-call resolution fallback chain (resolveJSNamespaceCallTargets -> resolveJSMergedDeclarationMemberTargets -> terminal-name resolveCallTargets) is copy-pasted in the per-symbol path (~line 2869) and the top-level path (~line 3264) with one subtle guard diff
--- 7. [CONFIRMED] cleanup internal/sem/provider.go:2115
S: forEachRelation's signature has grown to nine positional parameters (two of them nilable callbacks) with every cross-cutting concern added as another argument threaded through all callers
--- 8. [CONFIRMED] cleanup internal/sem/js_scopes.go:278
S: qualifiedNamespaceCall is recomputed per covering symbol for the same call site (nested symbol ranges like class + its methods overlap), and topLevelNamespaceCalls does an O(symbols) linear span scan per call instead of classifying each call once
--- 9. [CONFIRMED] cleanup internal/sem/parser.go:6420
S: javascriptVariableDeclaratorEnd calls stripCodeLiteralsAndComments on the entire file content once per exported-variable match instead of the caller stripping once
--- 10. [CONFIRMED] cleanup internal/sem/provider.go:2673
S: jsNamespaceBySymbolID runs unconditionally for every scanned JS/TS file, paying a full-file stripCodeLiteralsAndComments pass (plus per-symbol regex fallback compiles) even when the scan found zero namespaces, which is the common case

findings.summary: resolveJSMergedDeclarationMemberTargets returns (nil, true) when the merged same-file declaration has no member with the called name, suppressing the workspace fallback and dropping legitimate edges to members of the same namespace reopened in another file.
findings.failure_scenario: a.ts: `class A { static create() {} }` + `namespace A { export const marker = 1 }` + `caller() { A.g(); }`; b.ts: `namespace A { export function g() {} }`. The merged class has no member g, but matched=true suppresses the terminal-name fallback that resolves b.ts's A.g — verified differentially: remove the class and the edge appears. A round-3 fix regression.

findings.summary: bindingShadowsNamespaceReceiver finds no anchor scope when the matched prefix namespace exists only via dotted declarations (`namespace A.B`), so anchorStart stays -1 and an outer same-named binding wrongly shadows the namespace receiver.
findings.failure_scenario: `function B() {}` + `namespace A.B { export function f() {} }` + `namespace A.X { export function g() { B.f(); } }` — no scope named exactly "A" is recorded, the anchor loop finds nothing, and the file-level function B binding shadows the call; the CALLS edge A.X.g -> A.B.f silently disappears (empirically confirmed). A round-3 fix regression.

findings.summary: StreamSnapshot's recordFailure closure appends relation-phase jsScan failures without deduplicating against the entity-phase failure for the same file+code, so a fresh build double-counts what the cache-derived path dedupes.
findings.failure_scenario: A file exceeding the 5s parse budget in both phases yields two E_PARSE_TIMEOUT PartialFailures on a fresh build but one on a cache-derived selective snapshot — cache presence changes reported PartialFailures/stats, the exact invariant the PR claims to preserve.

findings.summary: Warm-preindex search now serves an OnlyFiles-confined selective view where main served the full graph, externalizing relation endpoints outside the candidate set. Working as designed — this is the PR's documented determinism contract (warm == cold results) — flagged for explicit design sign-off, not code change.
findings.failure_scenario: After `graph index`, a warm search whose candidates are src/caller.ts externalizes the edge to src/helper.ts that main's full-graph expansion resolved. Strict reduction of warm-path output vs main, in exchange for cache presence never changing results.

findings.summary: The regex scanner needed no parse budget; its replacement re-parses per file with a 5s timeout and on failure skips namespace-call classification for the file entirely (now observable as a warning PartialFailure, but edges are still lost).
findings.failure_scenario: A pathological file that parses in the entity phase but times out in the relation phase loses all namespace-qualified CALLS edges for that file — recorded, not silent, but an accepted trade-off worth an explicit decision.

findings.summary: The three-step namespace-call fallback chain is copy-pasted between the per-symbol (~2869) and top-level (~3264) paths with a subtle guard difference — the exact structure that bred this round's finding 1.
findings.failure_scenario: Divergent maintenance of duplicated resolution chains; extracting one shared helper removes the guard drift and shrinks the surface future reviews must attack.

findings.summary: forEachRelation has grown to nine positional parameters including two nilable callbacks threaded through all callers.
findings.failure_scenario: Every new cross-cutting concern adds a positional argument; a params struct would stop the growth and the nil-callback footguns.

findings.summary: qualifiedNamespaceCall is recomputed per covering symbol for the same call site (nested ranges overlap), and topLevelNamespaceCalls does an O(symbols) span scan per call instead of classifying each call once.
findings.failure_scenario: Redundant per-call work on files with deeply nested symbols; classify each call site once and attribute by containing range.

findings.summary: javascriptVariableDeclaratorEnd strips the entire file's literals/comments once per exported-variable match instead of the caller stripping once.
findings.failure_scenario: O(exports x file-size) redundant stripping on files with many exported variables.

findings.summary: jsNamespaceBySymbolID runs unconditionally per scanned JS/TS file, paying a full-file strip plus per-symbol regex fallbacks even when the scan found zero namespaces (the common case).
findings.failure_scenario: Wasted full-file pass on every namespace-free JS/TS file; gate it on the scan state having found namespaces.

content.content: 10 findings reported.

findings.summary: resolveJSMergedDeclarationMemberTargets returns (nil, true) when the merged same-file declaration has no member with the called name, suppressing the workspace fallback and dropping legitimate edges to members of the same namespace reopened in another file.
findings.failure_scenario: a.ts: `class A { static create() {} }` + `namespace A { export const marker = 1 }` + `caller() { A.g(); }`; b.ts: `namespace A { export function g() {} }`. The merged class has no member g, but matched=true suppresses the terminal-name fallback that resolves b.ts's A.g — verified differentially: remove the class and the edge appears. A round-3 fix regression.

findings.summary: bindingShadowsNamespaceReceiver finds no anchor scope when the matched prefix namespace exists only via dotted declarations (`namespace A.B`), so anchorStart stays -1 and an outer same-named binding wrongly shadows the namespace receiver.
findings.failure_scenario: `function B() {}` + `namespace A.B { export function f() {} }` + `namespace A.X { export function g() { B.f(); } }` — no scope named exactly "A" is recorded, the anchor loop finds nothing, and the file-level function B binding shadows the call; the CALLS edge A.X.g -> A.B.f silently disappears (empirically confirmed). A round-3 fix regression.

findings.summary: StreamSnapshot's recordFailure closure appends relation-phase jsScan failures without deduplicating against the entity-phase failure for the same file+code, so a fresh build double-counts what the cache-derived path dedupes.
findings.failure_scenario: A file exceeding the 5s parse budget in both phases yields two E_PARSE_TIMEOUT PartialFailures on a fresh build but one on a cache-derived selective snapshot — cache presence changes reported PartialFailures/stats, the exact invariant the PR claims to preserve.

findings.summary: Warm-preindex search now serves an OnlyFiles-confined selective view where main served the full graph, externalizing relation endpoints outside the candidate set. Working as designed — this is the PR's documented determinism contract (warm == cold results) — flagged for explicit design sign-off, not code change.
findings.failure_scenario: After `graph index`, a warm search whose candidates are src/caller.ts externalizes the edge to src/helper.ts that main's full-graph expansion resolved. Strict reduction of warm-path output vs main, in exchange for cache presence never changing results.

findings.summary: The regex scanner needed no parse budget; its replacement re-parses per file with a 5s timeout and on failure skips namespace-call classification for the file entirely (now observable as a warning PartialFailure, but edges are still lost).
findings.failure_scenario: A pathological file that parses in the entity phase but times out in the relation phase loses all namespace-qualified CALLS edges for that file — recorded, not silent, but an accepted trade-off worth an explicit decision.

findings.summary: The three-step namespace-call fallback chain is copy-pasted between the per-symbol (~2869) and top-level (~3264) paths with a subtle guard difference — the exact structure that bred this round's finding 1.
findings.failure_scenario: Divergent maintenance of duplicated resolution chains; extracting one shared helper removes the guard drift and shrinks the surface future reviews must attack.

findings.summary: forEachRelation has grown to nine positional parameters including two nilable callbacks threaded through all callers.
findings.failure_scenario: Every new cross-cutting concern adds a positional argument; a params struct would stop the growth and the nil-callback footguns.

findings.summary: qualifiedNamespaceCall is recomputed per covering symbol for the same call site (nested ranges overlap), and topLevelNamespaceCalls does an O(symbols) span scan per call instead of classifying each call once.
findings.failure_scenario: Redundant per-call work on files with deeply nested symbols; classify each call site once and attribute by containing range.

findings.summary: javascriptVariableDeclaratorEnd strips the entire file's literals/comments once per exported-variable match instead of the caller stripping once.
findings.failure_scenario: O(exports x file-size) redundant stripping on files with many exported variables.

findings.summary: jsNamespaceBySymbolID runs unconditionally per scanned JS/TS file, paying a full-file strip plus per-symbol regex fallbacks even when the scan found zero namespaces (the common case).
findings.failure_scenario: Wasted full-file pass on every namespace-free JS/TS file; gate it on the scan state having found namespaces.

input.description: Fix round-4 findings
input.prompt:
You are fixing round-4 code-review findings in /Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph (Go repo, branch feat/retrieval-followups, working tree clean). Files you may edit: internal/sem/provider.go, internal/sem/js_scopes.go, internal/sem/typescript_test.go. Do NOT commit or git add.

FINDING 1 (provider.go ~1811, resolveJSMergedDeclarationMemberTargets): It returns (nil, true) when the merged same-file declaration (class/enum/object merged with a namespace name) has NO member with the called name — suppressing the terminal-name workspace fallback and dropping legitimate edges to members of the same namespace reopened in ANOTHER file. Verified repro: a.ts `class A { static create() {} }` + `namespace A { export const marker = 1 }` + `export function caller() { A.g(); }`; b.ts `namespace A { export function g() {} }` — main emits caller->A.g (cross-file), branch emits nothing; removing the class restores the edge. Fix the semantics: matched=true ONLY when a member target is actually found. When the merged declaration lacks the member, allow fallback — but preserve the original protection: the fallback for a merged receiver must prefer namespace-member/qualified resolution across files over bare-name workspace resolution; only suppress the bare-name fallback when it would resolve to a symbol unrelated to the receiver's namespace (study how the round-3 test TestTypeScriptMergedClassNamespaceStaticCallResolvesToClassMethod pins the phantom-edge case, and keep it passing). Add the cross-file repro as a test.

FINDING 2 (js_scopes.go ~228, bindingShadowsNamespaceReceiver): When the matched prefix namespace exists only via dotted declarations (`namespace A.B` — scopes recorded as "A.B", never "A"), the anchor loop finds no scope named exactly the prefix, anchorStart stays -1, and an outer same-named binding wrongly shadows the receiver. Verified repro: `function B() { return 1 }` + `namespace A.B { export function f() {} }` + `namespace A.X { export function g() { B.f(); } }` — TypeScript resolves B inside A.X to sibling namespace A.B; branch returns no call (qualifiedNamespaceCall ""), removing the function restores it. Fix: anchor resolution must treat dotted declarations as implicitly declaring each ancestor path segment — either register implicit ancestor scopes when walking dotted names (namespace A.B declares A with the same scope range), or make the anchor loop match any scope whose full path equals the prefix OR starts with prefix+"." (choose the design that keeps the round-3 tests TestTypeScriptNearerNamespaceBeatsOuterBindingShadow and its control passing). Add the repro as a test.

FINDING 4 (provider.go ~989, StreamSnapshot recordFailure): The streaming path appends relation-phase jsScan failures without deduplicating against the entity-phase failure for the same file+code, while the cache-derived selective path dedupes via mergePartialFailures — so a fresh build reports duplicates and cache presence changes reported PartialFailures. Fix: dedupe by code+file on the streaming/fresh-build path too (reuse mergePartialFailures or its key logic wherever failures are accumulated — check BuildProviderSnapshotWithOptions as well). Test: a file failing in both phases yields exactly one PartialFailure record per code+file on a fresh build.

FINDING 6 (simplification, provider.go ~2869 and ~3264): The three-step namespace-call fallback chain (resolveJSNamespaceCallTargets -> resolveJSMergedDeclarationMemberTargets -> terminal-name resolveCallTargets) is copy-pasted between the per-symbol and top-level paths with a subtle guard difference. Extract ONE shared helper used by both call sites (parameterize the per-symbol-only guards explicitly, e.g. the terminal != from.Name check), so the finding-1 fix lands in exactly one place. Preserve behavior of both sites apart from the finding-1 fix.

FINDING 10 (efficiency, provider.go ~2673): jsNamespaceBySymbolID runs unconditionally per scanned JS/TS file even when the scan state found zero namespaces. Gate it (empty scan state namespaces => skip / return empty map) — verify no consumer relies on entries for namespace-less files.

Do NOT touch the warm-path confinement design or the parse-timeout trade-off (accepted design). Keep ALL existing tests passing (especially all round-1/2/3 regression tests). Verify: gofmt -s (gofmt -l internal/sem clean), go vet ./internal/sem/, full go test -count=1 ./internal/sem/ and ./internal/cli/. Return raw facts: per-finding fix, tests added, results, deviations.

content.text:
Round-4 remediation is running: the two confirmed regressions (merged-declaration fallback suppression, dotted-namespace anchor gap), the partial-failure dedup invariant, plus the fallback-chain extraction so both call paths share one implementation — the duplication with a "subtle guard difference" is precisely what let this round's bug hide — and the cheap namespace-map gating.

Two findings from this round need your sign-off rather than code, and I've marked them as such in the report:

1. **Warm-path confinement** (flagged again, as in round 3): with a preindex, search now serves query-confined graphs where `main` served the full graph — endpoints outside the candidate set become externals. This is the PR's documented contract (cache presence can never change results), but it *is* a strict reduction of warm-path output vs `main`. If you'd rather keep repository-wide expansion on warm hits, that's a design reversal to decide explicitly, not a bug fix.
2. **Relation-phase parse budget**: a file that parses in the entity phase but times out in the relation phase loses its namespace-call edges for that run — now recorded as a warning-level partial failure rather than silent, which I consider the right trade-off, but it's a behavior `main`'s regex scanner didn't have.

When the agent lands: verify, re-cut, push, and a final focused verification of just these fix diffs — then my merge recommendation with the full four-round audit trail.

<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>All five findings fixed on `feat/retrieval-followups`; only the three permitted files touched, nothing committed.

## Finding 1 — merged-declaration member miss no longer swallows cross-file namespace edges
`/Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph/internal/sem/provider.go`
- `resolveJSMergedDeclarationMemberTargets` now returns `(targets, memberMatched, receiverMerged)`: `memberMatched` is true only when a member actually matched (unique → resolved; ambiguous multi-match → still true, stays unresolved by design); zero matches now allow fallback while still signaling `receiverMerged`.
- The fallback for a merged receiver is namespace-confined: candidates from `symbolsByShortName[terminal]` are kept only if a cross-file scan proves they sit in a namespace whose canonical path equals the receiver (`jsCrossFileNamespaceLookup` — lazy, memoized per file, uses `newJSScanState` + `jsNamespaceBySymbolID`; parse failure/no namespaces → ""). Unique related match → edge (confidence 0.8, `name_only`/`workspace`); zero or ambiguous → no edge, so the bare-name workspace fallback stays suppressed exactly when it would hit an unrelated symbol. Round-3 pin `TestTypeScriptMergedClassNamespaceStaticCallResolvesToClassMethod` still passes (member found → matched, no fallback).

## Finding 2 — dotted declarations anchor their ancestor prefixes
`/Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph/internal/sem/js_scopes.go`, `bindingShadowsNamespaceReceiver`: the anchor loop now matches any scope whose `Name == prefix` OR `strings.HasPrefix(Name, prefix+".")` (innermost-span wins, unchanged). `namespace A.X` therefore anchors prefix "A", so the file-level `function B` no longer shadows the sibling `A.B` receiver. Round-3 `TestTypeScriptNearerNamespaceBeatsOuterBindingShadow` (+ control) and `TestTypeScriptNestedDottedNamespaceQualifiesAgainstParent` pass unchanged.

## Finding 4 — fresh-build failure dedupe
`provider.go` `StreamSnapshot`: relation-phase failures now accumulate in `relationFailures` and merge via `failures = mergePartialFailures(failures, relationFailures)` — same code+file collapses, entity-phase record wins, matching the cache-derived selective path in `search_cache.go`. `BuildProviderSnapshotWithOptions` is a pure sink over `StreamSnapshot`, so it's covered; grep confirmed no other failure-accumulation sites.

## Finding 6 — one shared chain
New `resolveJSNamespaceCallChain(name, from, sameFile, namespaces, symbolsByShortName, importsByName, foreignNamespaceOf, skipTerminal)` in provider.go implements namespace → merged-member → terminal-fallback once. Per-symbol site passes `skipTerminal = terminal == from.Name || childNamesByContainer[from.ID][terminal]`; top-level site passes `nil` (its previous unguarded behavior). Both former copy-pasted blocks replaced.

## Finding 10 — gated per-file namespace mapping
`jsNamespaceBySymbolID` now runs only when `len(jsScan.namespaces) > 0`. Safe: with zero namespaces it returned an empty map anyway (verified `jsNamespaceAtByte/AtLine` return "" with no scopes), and all consumers are gated on `jsNamespaceCalls`, which is empty without namespaces; nil-map lookups are fine in Go.

## Tests added (internal/sem/typescript_test.go)
- `TestTypeScriptMergedDeclarationMissingMemberResolvesCrossFileNamespaceMember` — finding-1 repro: edge `caller -> g` lands in `src/b.ts` even with an unrelated `src/unrelated.ts g`; second subtest pins that with no namespace member anywhere, no bare-name edge is fabricated.
- `TestTypeScriptDottedNamespaceSiblingBeatsFileBindingShadow` — finding-2 repro: `g -> f` present; control `h` (top-level, genuinely shadowed) has no edge.
- `TestStreamSnapshotDedupesSameCodeFailuresAcrossPhases` — seam test: entity-phase E_PARSE_TIMEOUT + `jsScanPartialFailure` (asserted same code) collapse to one record with the entity record kept; distinct codes both survive.
- `TestStreamSnapshotFreshBuildReportsNoDuplicateFailures` — end-to-end fresh build where the same file fails both phases (entity E_PARSE_ERROR from a syntax error, relation E_PARSE_TIMEOUT via `jsScanParseTimeout=1ns`): both distinct-code records reported, no code+file pair duplicated.

**Deviation:** the exact "same code in both phases" scenario (E_PARSE_TIMEOUT twice) is not constructible end-to-end because the entity-phase budget `treeSitterParseTimeout` is a const in `parser.go` (a file I wasn't permitted to edit); the seam test pins that case with the literal records both phases produce, and the end-to-end test exercises the new merge line in `StreamSnapshot` with a real both-phase failure.

## Verification
- `gofmt -l internal/sem` — clean; `go vet ./internal/sem/` — clean.
- `go test -count=1 ./internal/sem/` — ok (32.2s, all round-1/2/3 regression tests included).
- `go test -count=1 ./internal/cli/` — ok.</result>

41	}

43	// loadOrBuildSearchGraphSnapshot preserves the exact candidate-file scope even
44	// when a complete committed-tree snapshot is published concurrently. The
45	// shared loader derives an OnlyFiles view from that full snapshot and reports a
46	// cache hit, so cache timing cannot change the graph search receives.
47	func loadOrBuildSearchGraphSnapshot(
48		ctx context.Context,
49		repo, providerVersion string,
50		options ProviderSnapshotOptions,
51		cacheDir string,
52		disableCache bool,
53	) (ProviderSnapshot, bool, error) {

55	}

58		ctx context.Context,
59		repo, providerVersion string,
60		options ProviderSnapshotOptions,
61		cacheDir string,
62	) (ProviderSnapshot, bool, error) {
63		if cacheDir == "" || options.Worktree {
64			return ProviderSnapshot{}, false, nil
65		}
66		absRepo, err := filepath.Abs(repo)
67		if err != nil {
68			return ProviderSnapshot{}, false, err
69		}

73		}

75		fullOptions := options
76		fullOptions.OnlyFiles = nil
77		if fullOptions.Profile == "" {
78			fullOptions.Profile = ProfileFull
79		}
80		fullKey, keyErr := searchSnapshotKey(absRepo, repositoryKey, providerVersion, commit, tree, fullOptions)
81		if keyErr != nil {
82			return ProviderSnapshot{}, false, keyErr
83		}
84		fullPath := filepath.Join(cacheDir, "search", searchSnapshotCacheVersion, fullKey+".json.gz")
85		cached, readErr := readSearchSnapshot(fullPath)
86		if readErr != nil || !validCachedSearchSnapshot(cached, repositoryKey, providerVersion, commit, tree, fullOptions) {
87			return ProviderSnapshot{}, false, nil
88		}
89		return cached.Snapshot, true, nil
90	}
91	
92	// loadOrBuildSearchSnapshot is the single search-snapshot cache pipeline: it
93	// resolves HEAD and the repository key once, serves a valid per-query cache
94	// entry first, otherwise derives a selective view from a complete
95	// committed-tree snapshot (the optional preloadedFull already in memory, then
96	// the on-disk complete entry) and persists it, and finally falls back to a
97	// fresh build. Derivation failures are soft so an optional cache can never
98	// break retrieval.
99	func loadOrBuildSearchSnapshot(
100		ctx context.Context,
101		repo, providerVersion string,
102		options ProviderSnapshotOptions,
103		cacheDir string,
104		disableCache bool,
105		preloadedFull *ProviderSnapshot,
106	) (ProviderSnapshot, bool, error) {
107		if options.Profile == "" {
108			options.Profile = ProfileFull
109		}
110		if disableCache || cacheDir == "" || options.Worktree {
111			snapshot, err := BuildProviderSnapshotWithOptions(ctx, repo, providerVersion, options)
112			return snapshot, false, err
113		}
114		absRepo, err := filepath.Abs(repo)
115		if err != nil {
116			return ProviderSnapshot{}, false, err
117		}
118		commit, tree, headErr := resolveCommittedHEAD(ctx, absRepo)
119		if headErr != nil {
120			snapshot, buildErr := BuildProviderSnapshotWithOptions(ctx, repo, providerVersion, options)
121			return snapshot, false, buildErr
122		}
123		repositoryKey := repoKey(ctx, absRepo)
124		key, err := searchSnapshotKey(absRepo, repositoryKey, providerVersion, commit, tree, options)
125		if err != nil {
126			return ProviderSnapshot{}, false, err
127		}
128		path := filepath.Join(cacheDir, "search", searchSnapshotCacheVersion, key+".json.gz")
129		if cached, err := readSearchSnapshot(path); err == nil && validCachedSearchSnapshot(cached, repositoryKey, providerVersion, commit, tree, options) {
130			return cached.Snapshot, true, nil
131		}
132		// A complete committed-tree snapshot is query independent and can serve a
133		// selective search without rebuilding the same tree for every query. Keep
134		// the selective view so cache presence cannot change retrieval semantics.
135		if len(options.OnlyFiles) > 0 {
136			deriveFromFull := func(full ProviderSnapshot) (ProviderSnapshot, bool) {
137				selective, deriveErr := selectiveSearchSnapshotFromFull(ctx, absRepo, providerVersion, options, full)
138				if deriveErr != nil {
139					// Provenance or internal-metadata mismatches make this complete
140					// snapshot unsuitable for derivation. Fall through instead of
141					// letting an optional cache break retrieval.
142					return ProviderSnapshot{}, false
143				}
144				// Persisting the exact selective view makes repeated identical queries
145				// a direct cache hit. As with ordinary search caching, this is best effort.
146				_ = writeSearchSnapshot(path, newCachedSearchSnapshot(providerVersion, commit, tree, options, selective))
147				return selective, true
148			}
149			if preloadedFull != nil {
150				if selective, ok := deriveFromFull(*preloadedFull); ok {
151					return selective, true, nil
152				}
153			}
154			fullOptions := options
155			fullOptions.OnlyFiles = nil
156			fullKey, keyErr := searchSnapshotKey(absRepo, repositoryKey, providerVersion, commit, tree, fullOptions)
157			if keyErr != nil {
158				return ProviderSnapshot{}, false, keyErr
159			}
160			fullPath := filepath.Join(cacheDir, "search", searchSnapshotCacheVersion, fullKey+".json.gz")
161			if cached, readErr := readSearchSnapshot(fullPath); readErr == nil && validCachedSearchSnapshot(cached, repositoryKey, providerVersion, commit, tree, fullOptions) {
162				if selective, ok := deriveFromFull(cached.Snapshot); ok {
163					return selective, true, nil
164				}
165			}
166		}
167		snapshot, err := BuildProviderSnapshotWithOptions(ctx, repo, providerVersion, options)
168		if err != nil {
169			return ProviderSnapshot{}, false, err
170		}
171		if searchSnapshotProvenanceChanged(snapshot.Header, commit, tree) {
172			return ProviderSnapshot{}, false, fmt.Errorf(
173				"HEAD changed while building search snapshot: got commit %q tree %q, started at commit %q tree %q",
174				snapshot.Header.Commit, snapshot.Header.Tree, commit, tree,
175			)
176		}
177		cache := newCachedSearchSnapshot(providerVersion, commit, tree, options, snapshot)
178		// Cache persistence is best effort. Retrieval correctness never depends on
179		// a writable cache directory.
180		_ = writeSearchSnapshot(path, cache)
181		return snapshot, false, nil
182	}

input.prompt:
READ-ONLY adversarial verification in /Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph (branch feat/retrieval-followups). Do not modify anything except files under /private/tmp used for probes; you may create throwaway test repos in /private/tmp and run the built binary or `go test`/`go run` probes that do not touch the repo working tree.

Target: the round-4 fix for merged-declaration namespace fallback in internal/sem/provider.go — resolveJSMergedDeclarationMemberTargets now returns (targets, memberMatched, receiverMerged), and the shared chain helper resolveJSNamespaceCallChain implements namespace -> merged-member -> terminal fallback, where a merged receiver's fallback is namespace-confined via jsCrossFileNamespaceLookup (lazy memoized cross-file parse, unique related match -> 0.8-confidence edge, ambiguous/zero -> no edge).

Try hard to BREAK it. Attack angles at minimum: (1) does the cross-file lookup fabricate edges when two files both reopen namespace A with a same-named member (ambiguity must yield no edge)? (2) does memoization key correctly per file, or can one file's parse result leak to another? (3) what happens when the cross-file candidate file fails to parse or exceeds the parse budget — fabricated or lost edges? (4) does the namespace-confined fallback wrongly reject a legitimate target whose namespace path differs by dotted-declaration spelling (namespace A.B vs nested namespace A { namespace B })? (5) non-merged receivers: did the chain refactor change per-symbol vs top-level guard behavior (skipTerminal) anywhere observable? (6) performance: is the lazy cross-file scan bounded, or can a workspace with many same-short-name symbols trigger O(files) parses per call? Construct real probes with BuildProviderSnapshotWithOptions or the test helpers in internal/sem — empirical evidence only.

Return: verdict per angle (BROKEN with repro, or HOLDS with the probe evidence), and an overall CONFIRMED-SOUND / DEFECT-FOUND conclusion. Raw facts.

READ-ONLY adversarial verification in /Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph (branch feat/retrieval-followups). Do not modify the repo; throwaway probes under /private/tmp or `go test -run` invocations are fine.

Target: the round-4 fix in internal/sem/js_scopes.go bindingShadowsNamespaceReceiver — the anchor loop now matches any namespace scope whose Name == prefix OR HasPrefix(Name, prefix+"."), innermost-span wins, so dotted-only declarations (namespace A.B) anchor their ancestor prefixes.

Try hard to BREAK it. Attack angles at minimum: (1) prefix collision: namespaces "A.B" and "AB.C" or "A" vs "AX" — does the prefix+"." guard prevent false anchors? (2) multiple disjoint scopes matching the prefix (namespace A.B at top and another namespace A.C later; or the same dotted path declared twice) — is the innermost/nearest anchor chosen relative to the CALL position, or can a far scope win and flip a shadow decision? (3) the anchor semantics vs genuine shadows: a binding INSIDE the anchor scope must still shadow — construct a parameter named B inside namespace A.X calling B.f() with sibling namespace A.B and confirm the parameter wins; (4) empty-prefix (top-level namespace receiver) behavior unchanged; (5) interplay with round-3's jsCanonicalNamespaceWithPrefix — can a call match prefix "A" via a scope "A.B" that is NOT an ancestor of the resolved target namespace, wrongly unshadowing? Use the exported test helpers / package tests in internal/sem (write probes as _test.go files in a COPY of the package under /private/tmp if needed, or use `go test -run` with temporary files via the existing test patterns — do NOT add files to the repo).

Return: verdict per angle (BROKEN with repro / HOLDS with evidence), overall CONFIRMED-SOUND / DEFECT-FOUND. Raw facts.

input.description: Verify failure-dedup and chain refactor
input.prompt:
READ-ONLY adversarial verification in /Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph (branch feat/retrieval-followups). Do not modify the repo; probes under /private/tmp or `go test -run` are fine.

Targets: (a) the round-4 partial-failure dedup — mergePartialFailures moved to internal/sem/provider.go, StreamSnapshot merges relation-phase jsScan failures via mergePartialFailures(failures, relationFailures); (b) the mechanical soundness of the resolveJSNamespaceCallChain extraction (both former call sites' behavior preserved).

Attack angles: (1) failure ORDER and stats: does merging after accumulation preserve the summary ordering/counts the completeness level derives from — construct a snapshot with entity-phase and relation-phase failures across multiple files and compare PartialFailures/stats between a fresh build and a cache-derived selective snapshot of the same state (the invariant: identical reports); (2) does the relation-phase failure recording double-fire when both the per-symbol and top-level scans hit the same unparseable file? (3) chain-refactor equivalence: diff the pre-refactor behavior by reading git history (the two call sites at the previous commit) and verify the skipTerminal parameterization reproduces the old guards exactly — especially the top-level site's previously unguarded behavior; construct a probe where terminal == from.Name at top level to check the guard difference is preserved, not accidentally unified; (4) the shim commit: confirm intermediate commit 093d733 builds and its no-op recordFailure cannot lose failures in any path that commit ships (it must only affect the derived-selective path that the final commit replaces).

Return: verdict per angle (BROKEN with repro / HOLDS with evidence), overall CONFIRMED-SOUND / DEFECT-FOUND. Raw facts.

content.text: The three focused verifiers are attacking the round-4 fix mechanisms empirically — cross-file lookup ambiguity/memoization/parse-failure behavior, anchor-prefix collisions and shadow semantics, and failure-dedup invariants plus chain-refactor equivalence (including the shim commit's soundness). Their verdicts are the last input before my merge recommendation. The branch state meanwhile: four commits, all building independently, tip tests green, pushed and mergeable, CI running.

<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>All probes complete; the repo working tree was never modified (probe tests ran via `go test -overlay` mapping a virtual file from the scratchpad). Verdicts per angle, all empirical:

## Verdict per attack angle

**(1) Ambiguity across two reopening files — HOLDS.** Probe: `src/a.ts` (merged `class A` + `namespace A`, `caller()` calls `A.g()`), plus `src/b.ts` and `src/c.ts` each reopening `namespace A { export function g() {} }`. Result: zero CALLS edges to either `g` (ambiguous → no edge). Control with only `b.ts` reopening: edge present at confidence 0.8, resolution `name_only`. Extra probes: a `B.A.g` candidate (`namespace B { namespace A { g } }`) and a bare top-level `g` are both filtered while the true `A.g` in another file still wins uniquely; a candidate file that *has* `namespace A` but declares `g` outside it is also rejected — `jsNamespaceBySymbolID` only maps symbols lexically inside a namespace scope.

**(2) Memoization keying — HOLDS.** Unit probe on `jsCrossFileNamespaceLookup` with a counting `contentReader`: each file read exactly once across repeated lookups; a symbol carrying file-b's ID but file-c's `FilePath` returns "" (cache is keyed `FilePath` → per-file `ID` map, no cross-file leak); failed reads are negatively memoized (1 read total); non-JS/TS symbols return "" without any read.

**(3) Parse failure / budget — HOLDS (conservative: loses, never fabricates).** Unreadable candidate file → chain returns nil (baseline with readable file returns the unique 0.8 edge, proving the probe discriminates). Genuine scope-parse timeout (1ns budget on a 2000-line candidate, timeout verified to actually trip via `newJSScanState` returning error) → nil, no fabrication. `nil` foreignNamespaceOf → nil. Snapshot-level: candidate file over `MaxParseBytes` (400) emits `E_FILE_TOO_LARGE` / "symbol parsing skipped", produces no symbols, hence can never be a candidate — no edge, failure surfaced. One raw fact: a *cross-file lookup* scope-parse timeout is silent (no PartialFailure is recorded from inside `jsCrossFileNamespaceLookup`, provider.go:1903-1920) — a lost edge with no observability, but never a wrong edge.

**(4) Dotted vs nested spelling — HOLDS.** Caller merged on receiver `A.B` (`class B` inside `namespace A`, plus `namespace A.B` reopening); candidate declared as `namespace A.B { g }` and as `namespace A { namespace B { g } }` both resolve to the edge, and an unrelated bare `g` never steals it. Both spellings canonicalize to `"A.B"` (`jsNamespaceDeclarationName` joins/trims `nested_identifier`; `jsQualifiedNamespaceName` prefixes nesting).

**(5) skipTerminal / non-merged behavior — HOLDS, one raw fact.** Per-symbol path: `A.g()` inside `function g` with the member reopened cross-file emits no edge (self-name terminal suppressed — matches the documented intent and the bare-name path convention; a small recall loss by design). Top-level path (`skipTerminal == nil`): `A.g();` at file level emits `from=local/001:file:src/a.ts conf=0.8 res=name_only`. Raw fact: for a **non-merged** receiver (`namespace A` with only `marker`, no merged class), `A.g()` still falls through to the generic resolver and resolves workspace-wide to an unrelated bare `g` at `conf=0.68 res=name_only` ("matched globally unique symbol name") — that is the pre-existing reopened-namespace fallback the fix deliberately left in place (confinement applies only when a merged declaration proves the receiver is not a plain identifier); it is not a regression from this fix, but it is a fabrication vector the merged-receiver path closes and the non-merged path does not.

**(6) Performance bound — HOLDS.** Probe with 41 candidate files sharing short name `g`: first chain call reads/parses each candidate file exactly once (41 reads); a second identical call performs zero additional reads (memoized for the lifetime of one `forEachRelation` pass — the closure at provider.go:2278 is built once per relation phase). A chain that short-circuits at the merged-member step performs zero reads (fully lazy). Worst case is one extra scope parse per distinct JS/TS file containing a same-short-name candidate, per snapshot build — O(files) total, not O(files) per call. Concurrency check: no goroutines anywhere in the relation phase (`forEachRelation` body has zero `go func`/`sync.WaitGroup`/`errgroup` uses), and each `forEachRelation` invocation gets its own cache closure, so the unsynchronized map is safe.

## Overall: CONFIRMED-SOUND

No defect found in the round-4 code. All existing repo tests in the area (`TestTypeScriptMerged*`, `TestTypeScriptDottedNamespace*`, timeout test) also pass on HEAD (`feat/retrieval-followups`, 8eb5b9a). Two non-defect observations worth relaying: (a) silent (unrecorded) partial failure when a cross-file candidate's scope parse times out inside `jsCrossFileNamespaceLookup`; (b) the non-merged-receiver terminal fallback still permits a 0.68 workspace-wide `name_only` edge to an unrelated same-named function — pre-existing behavior, outside this fix's scope.

Key files: `/Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph/internal/sem/provider.go` (lines 1787-1926, 2278, 2997, 3375), `/Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph/internal/sem/js_scopes.go`. Probe file (throwaway): `/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-thomi-Projects-entire-graph/fffff083-8032-496a-81ae-244c3c25ea0f/scratchpad/zz_adversarial_probe_test.go` run with `overlay.json` in the same directory.</result>

content.text:
First verifier verdict: **CONFIRMED-SOUND** — all six attack angles on the merged-declaration fallback held, with genuinely adversarial probes (multi-file ambiguity, memoization leak attempts, forced parse timeouts, dotted-vs-nested spellings, a 41-file performance bound). Two worthwhile non-defect observations for the record: a cross-file candidate's scope-parse timeout loses an edge silently (conservative but unobservable), and non-merged receivers retain the pre-existing 0.68-confidence workspace fallback — a fabrication vector that predates this PR and is out of its scope.

Two verifiers remain (anchor-prefix shadows; failure-dedup and chain equivalence). Final report when they land.

<tool-use-id>toolu_012LQPRQeu7v73cwAjLtZPFb</tool-use-id>
<output-file>/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-thomi-Projects-entire-graph/fffff083-8032-496a-81ae-244c3c25ea0f/tasks/adeddeeb84a408505.output</output-file>
<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Verify anchor-prefix shadow fix" finished</summary>
<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>All probes pass and the entire existing sem test suite (40s, full package) passes with the outermost anchor — confirming both the diagnosis and that the existing round-4 tests never covered the distinguishing case.

## Verdict

**Overall: DEFECT-FOUND** — in `bindingShadowsNamespaceReceiver`, `/Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph/internal/sem/js_scopes.go` lines 231-255. The innermost-span anchor choice is wrong; the correct anchor is the **outermost** containing scope matching the prefix.

### Per-angle results

**(1) Prefix collision — HOLDS.** The `scope.Name != prefix && !strings.HasPrefix(scope.Name, prefix+".")` guard correctly rejects `AB.C` for prefix `A` and `A.BC.D` for prefix `A.B` (verified by direct unit calls on synthetic states plus a realistic collision file; shadow decisions unaffected). Canonical nested naming (`jsQualifiedNamespaceName` makes nested decls parent-relative) also makes textual collisions within one containment chain unconstructible from real source.

**(2) Multiple disjoint scopes — HOLDS for disjoint, BROKEN for nested.** Disjoint scopes are handled: the `position <= scope.startByte || position >= scope.endByte` filter (line 239) means a far reopened `namespace A.B` can never anchor a call it doesn't contain (probe with A.B reopened before and after the call site passed). But among **nested** matching scopes, innermost-span picks the wrong anchor — see defect below.

**(3) Binding inside anchor still shadows — HOLDS for the asked case, BROKEN one level out.** Parameter `B` inside `namespace A.X` calling `B.f()` with sibling `namespace A.B` correctly shadows (tsc ground truth: parameter wins; code returns ""). But a binding in the region **between the outer and inner matching scopes' starts** fails to shadow — the defect.

**(4) Empty prefix — HOLDS.** `anchorStart = -1`, all enclosing bindings shadow; `N.f()` qualifies at top level, parameter `N` shadows. Unchanged.

**(5) Non-ancestor scope match — HOLDS.** Call inside `namespace A.B` resolving `Sub.target()` to `A.Sub` (not an ancestor of A.B) with a file-level `const Sub` outside: code does not shadow, produces the edge — and tsc agrees (ran it: prints `NAMESPACE A.Sub.target`; the merged namespace member is lexically closer than the file-level const).

### The defect (repro)

```ts
namespace A.Sub { export function target() {} }
namespace A.B {
  const Sub = { target() {} };
  export namespace C {
    export function caller() { Sub.target(); }   // tsc executes the LOCAL const
  }
}
```
Ground truth (tsc 6.0.3 compiled + executed under node): prints `LOCAL const Sub`. The Go code fabricates `A.Sub.target`: matching containing scopes are `A.B` [64,181) and `A.B.C` [118,179); innermost-span picks anchorStart=118; the `const Sub` binding scope starts at 64 (A.B's body) → `64 >= 118` fails → not shadowed. End-to-end via `BuildProviderSnapshot`: phantom relation `CALLS caller→target, Confidence:0.92, Resolution:"exact"`.

Worse, it's a **regression of the exact-name path**, not just a gap in the new dotted support: with outer scope named exactly `A` (`namespace A { const Sub = ...; export namespace B { caller } }`), the nested `A.B` scope steals the anchor from the exact-name `A` scope via smaller span. tsc: local const wins; code fabricates `A.Sub.target`. Pre-round-4 exact-name-only matching would have anchored at `A` and gotten this right.

### Fix validation

Replacing smallest-span with earliest-start (outermost matching containing scope) in a scratch copy makes both failing probes pass, keeps all 6 other probes passing, and the **full `internal/sem` test suite passes** (`go test ./internal/sem`, ok in 40s). Rationale: prefix-namespace members are visible from the outermost enclosing scope whose path extends the prefix, so every binding declared at-or-inside that scope is lexically nearer and must shadow; innermost anchoring wrongly exempts bindings declared between the outer and inner scope starts. Among containing scopes only nested chains exist (disjoint scopes are filtered by containment), so "innermost" buys nothing and only loses these cases.

No repo files were modified; all probes ran in a copy under the scratchpad.</result>

**(1) Prefix collision — HOLDS.** The `scope.Name != prefix && !strings.HasPrefix(scope.Name, prefix+".")` guard correctly rejects `AB.C` for prefix `A` and `A.BC.D` for prefix `A.B` (verified by direct unit calls on synthetic states plus a realistic collision file; shadow decisions unaffected). Canonical nested naming (`jsQualifiedNamespaceName` makes nested decls parent-relative) also makes textual collisions within one containment chain unconstructible from real source.

**(2) Multiple disjoint scopes — HOLDS for disjoint, BROKEN for nested.** Disjoint scopes are handled: the `position <= scope.startByte || position >= scope.endByte` filter (line 239) means a far reopened `namespace A.B` can never anchor a call it doesn't contain (probe with A.B reopened before and after the call site passed). But among **nested** matching scopes, innermost-span picks the wrong anchor — see defect below.

**(3) Binding inside anchor still shadows — HOLDS for the asked case, BROKEN one level out.** Parameter `B` inside `namespace A.X` calling `B.f()` with sibling `namespace A.B` correctly shadows (tsc ground truth: parameter wins; code returns ""). But a binding in the region **between the outer and inner matching scopes' starts** fails to shadow — the defect.

**(4) Empty prefix — HOLDS.** `anchorStart = -1`, all enclosing bindings shadow; `N.f()` qualifies at top level, parameter `N` shadows. Unchanged.

**(5) Non-ancestor scope match — HOLDS.** Call inside `namespace A.B` resolving `Sub.target()` to `A.Sub` (not an ancestor of A.B) with a file-level `const Sub` outside: code does not shadow, produces the edge — and tsc agrees (ran it: prints `NAMESPACE A.Sub.target`; the merged namespace member is lexically closer than the file-level const).

### The defect (repro)

```ts
namespace A.Sub { export function target() {} }
namespace A.B {
  const Sub = { target() {} };
  export namespace C {
    export function caller() { Sub.target(); }   // tsc executes the LOCAL const
  }
}
```
Ground truth (tsc 6.0.3 compiled + executed under node): prints `LOCAL const Sub`. The Go code fabricates `A.Sub.target`: matching containing scopes are `A.B` [64,181) and `A.B.C` [118,179); innermost-span picks anchorStart=118; the `const Sub` binding scope starts at 64 (A.B's body) → `64 >= 118` fails → not shadowed. End-to-end via `BuildProviderSnapshot`: phantom relation `CALLS caller→target, Confidence:0.92, Resolution:"exact"`.

Worse, it's a **regression of the exact-name path**, not just a gap in the new dotted support: with outer scope named exactly `A` (`namespace A { const Sub = ...; export namespace B { caller } }`), the nested `A.B` scope steals the anchor from the exact-name `A` scope via smaller span. tsc: local const wins; code fabricates `A.Sub.target`. Pre-round-4 exact-name-only matching would have anchored at `A` and gotten this right.

### Fix validation

Replacing smallest-span with earliest-start (outermost matching containing scope) in a scratch copy makes both failing probes pass, keeps all 6 other probes passing, and the **full `internal/sem` test suite passes** (`go test ./internal/sem`, ok in 40s). Rationale: prefix-namespace members are visible from the outermost enclosing scope whose path extends the prefix, so every binding declared at-or-inside that scope is lexically nearer and must shadow; innermost anchoring wrongly exempts bindings declared between the outer and inner scope starts. Among containing scopes only nested chains exist (disjoint scopes are filtered by containment), so "innermost" buys nothing and only loses these cases.

No repo files were modified; all probes ran in a copy under the scratchpad.</result>
